  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts --test-name-pattern "receipt hash helpers"`
  and `node tools\launch\run-template-receipt-helper.js examples/template/ai-sdk-receipt-hashes.ts --check --json`.
  This remains source-only helper proof; no provider credentials, model
  streaming, browser runtime, server, build, or deploy proof was run.

- Lane 7 dashboard score-scope cleanup, 2026-05-23: the default dashboard now
  labels Lane 7 aggregate and per-package readiness numbers with explicit
  `data-dx-score-scope` markers, so UI/Motion/3D/WASM package scores cannot be
  confused with the capped Forge launch score. The materializer refreshed the
  checked-in source template and browser-preview dashboard copies. Verification:
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\lane7-default-template-surface.test.ts`
  plus green focused launch score/reality/source-honesty/AI truth cluster. This
  is source and generated-template honesty only; browser, WebGL, WASM execution,
  live provider credentials, broad builds, and deploys remain unclaimed.

- Launch package catalog honesty-label closure, 2026-05-23: every
  `launchPackageCatalog` entry now carries an explicit `honestyLabel`, so
  adapter-boundary, source-only, lock-backed source-owned, real, partial, and
  blocked surfaces cannot silently omit maturity truth. This is catalog/source
  honesty only; it does not add browser runtime proof, provider credentials, or
  live package execution. Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`.
  Receipt-helper checks found existing stale receipt inputs in
  `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` and legacy CommonJS helper
  runner drift under the template `type: module`; those remain separate
  launch-hardening work.

- DX Build App Router execution-contract guard, 2026-05-23: the installed
  `dx build` smoke now parses `.dx/build/app/app-router-execution.json`
  instead of accepting file existence as proof. It rejects wrong root route
  metadata, missing `node_modules_present=false`, local node_modules presence,
  and external framework runtime clone parity claims in that contract. Verification:
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`.
  This is installed-smoke contract hardening only; installed-default binary
  refresh, live route execution, broad builds, local servers, deploys, and full
  external framework clone parity remain skipped.

- Launch AI provider-readiness hardening, 2026-05-23: the default
  `app/api/ai/chat` route, generated-starter materializer, and checked-in
  browser-preview mirror now report provider credentials as readiness-only
  evidence. The configured-env branch uses
  `provider-configured-readiness-only`, dynamic `credentialsConfigured`,
  `runtimeProof: false`, and `liveProviderProof: false`, so a supplied env var
  cannot be mistaken for live model streaming or provider runtime proof. The
  launch package source-honesty guard now scopes catalog entry parsing to the
  real `launchPackageCatalog` array before checking explicit honesty labels.
  Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-launch-visible-proof.test.ts
  .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`. Skipped by policy:
  browser runtime proof, live AI provider credentials, broad builds, local
  servers, and deploys.

- AI extended route inventory closure, 2026-05-23: the launch AI SDK boundary
  now inventories every non-chat `app/api/ai/*` route as outside the default
  proof surface, including text stream, UI stream, and file upload. The
  generated dashboard/package row lists those routes as provider-boundary
  endpoints while `/launch` stays focused on the default chat readiness
  workflow. Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-launch-visible-proof.test.ts --test-name-pattern
  "extended AI provider routes"` after rematerializing
  `examples/template` and `.dx/template-app-browser-preview`. No live
  provider credentials, model streaming, browser runtime proof, broad build,
  server, or deploy was run.

- App Router data-loader discovery, 2026-05-23: the public `DataLoader`
  registry now scans `app/**/page.{tsx,jsx,ts,js,html}` alongside legacy
  `pages/**/*.html`, maps App Router route groups and dynamic segments to
  route paths, and keeps non-page files out of loader registration. The
  project scanner also dropped the now-redundant `PAGE_EXTENSION` / `"html"`
  duplicate branch created by the launch extension cleanup. Verification:
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt. Targeted Cargo proof was attempted, but the fresh
  rerun timed out on the busy workspace while another Cargo job was active, so
  compiled proof for this exact final diff remains unclaimed.

- Launch dashboard score-scope hardening, 2026-05-23: generated package-row
  scores in the Forge reality table now carry
  `data-dx-score-scope="package-readiness-row"` and `data-dx-package-score`,
  so package readiness values such as `87/100` cannot be confused with the
  global 89/100 launch gate. Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-score-gate.test.ts --test-name-pattern "launch route copy"`,
  followed by the focused launch truth cluster. This is source and generated
  template hardening only; browser route proof, live providers, broad builds,
  local servers, and deploys remain governed separately.

- App API route-handler extension parity, 2026-05-23: the dev/runtime App
  Router API matcher now accepts `route.tsx`, `route.js`, and `route.jsx`
  beside `route.ts`, matching the source-build discovery contract for
  Next-familiar route handlers. It also ignores App Router route-group folders
  such as `app/api/(internal)/status/route.tsx` when matching `/api/status`.
  Dynamic and catch-all route params continue to flow through the source-owned
  request map without requiring `node_modules`.
  Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`, green focused
  `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli
  route_handler_match_accepts_next_route_handler_extensions --lib`, green
  focused `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli
  route_handler_match_ignores_app_router_route_groups --lib`, green
  route-handler interpreter source guard, and touched-file rustfmt. Cargo still
  emitted unrelated dirty-tree warnings in `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` and
  `dx-www/src/project.rs`; broad builds, local servers, installed-binary
  refresh, deploys, and live route-handler HTTP proof were skipped.

- App Router request-value decoding parity, 2026-05-23: the source-owned page
  and API route matchers now share `route_request_values` so dynamic and
  catch-all params are percent-decoded, query values decode `%xx`, and query
  `+` maps to spaces without requiring a Next.js or Node runtime. Invalid
  percent pairs are preserved instead of silently dropping request text. This
  is request-map parity only; RSC semantics, external framework route-handler execution,
  live HTTP proof, broad builds, and external bundler HMR remain unclaimed.
  Verification: red/green focused `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features
  --features cli decodes_request_params_and_search_params --lib`, green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`, and
  leaf-file rustfmt. The broader route-handler readiness guard currently fails
  on an unrelated Better Auth template receipt expectation, and
  `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` rustfmt was intentionally not applied because it
  attempted to rewrite unrelated active-worker formatting across the giant CLI
  module.

- DX Build server-data route-contract guard, 2026-05-23: the installed
  `dx build` smoke now rejects `.dx/build/app/server-data.json` when it does
  not describe the root `/` route from `app/page.tsx`, and it verifies the
  `loadLaunchMetrics` value materializes the expected launch route-handler
  count. Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`. This is build-output
  contract hardening only; live route execution, installed-default binary
  refresh, broad builds, local servers, deploys, and complete Next runtime parity remain
  skipped.

- dx-style HWB color fallback PostCSS canary, 2026-05-23 RUN 25:
  `related-crates/style` now emits readable `rgb/rgba` fallbacks for simple
  `hwb()` colors in the Rust-native PostCSS compatibility layer, while keeping
  the authored HWB declaration in the generated CSS. The matrix/fixture row
  `hwb-color-fallbacks` is source-guarded beside the existing import,
  nesting, custom-media, range-media, selector, logical, color-mix, gradient,
  page-break, sourcemap, and prefix canaries. Verification: red/green focused
  Rust canary in an isolated target and green focused
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with
  15/15 tests passing. This is official DX-starter PostCSS replacement proof;
  arbitrary plugin APIs, full color-space conversion, live PostCSS execution,
  and universal Autoprefixer parity remain unclaimed.

- Launch route-probe database readiness promotion, 2026-05-23:
  `/api/database-api/readiness` now counts as executable source-owned 200
  evidence in `tools/launch-stabilize/route-probe.cjs` and the coordinator
  instead of remaining an expected 501 adapter-boundary route. The route probe
  accepts `--include-database-readiness`; the old boundary-named flag is kept
  only as a compatibility alias to the same 200 proof. Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-stabilize-route-probe.test.cjs
  .\benchmarks\launch-stabilize-coordinator.test.cjs`. The coordinator
  dirty-file owner map now also classifies current parser-extension,
  route-handler, Forge/package, Flow Forge, launch-status, and dx-build
  handoff paths instead of blocking them as unassigned. This does not claim live
  port-3000 proof, provider database credentials, installed-binary freshness,
  or full route-handler parity.

- Launch report receipt-boundary cleanup, 2026-05-23: generated
  `launch-adoption-report` and `launch-manifest-drift` contracts now write to
  `.dx/forge/reports/*.json` instead of looking like checked-in
  `.dx/forge/template-readiness` source receipts. The template-readiness guard
  now scans CLI/materializer sources too, so missing readiness receipt
  references cannot hide in launch contracts. Verification:
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-template-readiness-receipts.test.ts`.
  This is source-contract cleanup only; it does not run browser, provider,
  build, or deploy proof.

- Launch package catalog maturity naming guard, 2026-05-23: every
  `examples/template/package-catalog.ts` entry now has a DX-facing
  official package name, and the maturity type includes the lock-backed
  adapter-boundary classification used by Authentication. Verification:
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`.
  This remains source-contract proof only; browser, runtime, provider, deploy,
  and broad build proof are still governed separately.

- Template-readiness receipt closure, 2026-05-23: launch template readiness
  references now resolve to checked-in no-execution receipts instead of
  pointing at missing files. Added source and browser-preview copies for the
  launch route, readiness bundle, runtime checklist, runtime approval request,
  runtime evidence stub, verification lane, companion docs, Zed handoff, and
  3D scene readiness. Verification:
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-template-readiness-receipts.test.ts`.
  These receipts are source-level proof only and keep browser, provider,
  deploy, and production runtime evidence pending behind explicit approval.

- DX Build proof-breadth projection, 2026-05-23: the readiness gate now
  carries existing source-build content, MDX, CSS optimization/source-map, and
  image metadata counts through its report, consumer snapshot, readiness
  projection, and Zed handoff projection. This gives DX CLI, Zed, and Friday a
  compact machine-readable view of `dx build` proof breadth without parsing the
  full source-build receipt. Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`. This remains source receipt
  projection only; installed-binary refresh, local servers, broad builds,
  deploys, and governed runtime proof were skipped.

- dx-style mixed media range PostCSS canary, 2026-05-23 RUN 24: `related-crates/style` now lowers mixed strict/inclusive media ranges without a JS PostCSS chain. Starter CSS such as `(48rem < width <= 72rem)` emits `(min-width: calc(48rem + 0.02px)) and (max-width: 72rem)`, and `(30rem <= height < 50rem)` emits `(min-height: 30rem) and (max-height: calc(50rem - 0.02px))`. The matrix/fixture row `mixed-media-range-syntax` is now source-guarded beside the existing import, nesting, custom-media, strict range-media, selector, color, logical, and prefix canaries. Verification: red/green focused Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 14/14 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo/workspace builds, local servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, cascade-layer flattening, and universal Autoprefixer parity.

- Data-loader launch extension cleanup, 2026-05-23: legacy pages data-loader
  discovery now scans `.html` launch page source instead of stale `.pg` files,
  and the public developer contract no longer lists `.pg` / `.cp` in allowed
  source globs. This is source-discovery cleanup only; App Router loader
  parity, full `dx build` proof, and governed runtime validation remain
  separate. Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs`.

- 3D Scene System receipt freshness repair, 2026-05-23: the lock-backed
  `3d/launch-scene` package lane now has current SHA-256 mirrors for the
  edited launch runtime asset in package-status, the typed read model, and
  package receipts. This is source/receipt freshness only; it does not claim
  browser WebGL, screenshot, renderer install, or provider proof. Verification:
  red/green 3D subset of `launch-forge-package-status-read-model`, green
  `three-scene-receipt-hash-refresh`, helper `--check --json`, and focused
  launch truth cluster.

- DX Build readiness route-handler projection, 2026-05-23: the readiness gate
  now preserves source-build `route_handlers` in its receipt summary and writes
  that count into generated `dx.build.readiness` and `dx.build.zedHandoff`
  projections. The quality guard also tracks the new route-handler installed
  smoke helper modules, keeping the proof path split instead of drifting back
  into a monolith. Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`. This is still source
  projection evidence only; installed-binary refresh, local servers, broad
  builds, deploys, and governed runtime proof were skipped.

- DX Build installed-smoke graph-node proof, 2026-05-23: the installed smoke
  harness now reads `.dx/receipts/graph/latest.json` and fails if the tiny
  `app/api/health/route.ts` fixture is not represented as an
  `app-route-handler` node with `GET`, the source-owned route-handler
  execution contract, `node_modules_required=false`, and no lifecycle script
  execution. Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`. This remains source/candidate
  smoke evidence only; installed-default refresh, live handler execution,
  broad builds, local servers, deploys, and complete Next runtime parity were skipped.

- DX Build installed-smoke graph schema guard, 2026-05-23: the installed smoke
  now rejects `.dx/receipts/graph/latest.json` unless its schema is exactly
  `dx.build.graph`, and it now applies the same exact-schema requirement to
  `dx.www.sourceBuildManifest`, both source-build receipt aliases, and
  `dx.build.zedHandoff`. This prevents graph-shaped, source-build-shaped, or
  editor-handoff-shaped experimental receipts from passing as launch build
  evidence. Verification: red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`. This is receipt-contract
  hardening only; installed-default refresh, live route-handler execution,
  broad builds, servers, deploys, and complete Next runtime parity remain skipped.

- Public extension constant cleanup, 2026-05-23: `dx-www` now exports
  launch source constants for `.html` pages and `.tsx` components instead of
  the stale internal `.pg` / `.cp` names. This closes a public API mismatch
  only; it does not claim full `dx build` end-to-end runtime proof or remove
  every legacy parser/data path. Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs`.

- dx-style custom property `var()` fallback PostCSS canary, 2026-05-23 RUN 23: `related-crates/style` now closes another real PostCSS preset-env/custom-properties gap without a JS PostCSS chain. `var()` fallback handling now substitutes the full declaration value, so `border: 1px solid var(--brand)` emits `border: 1px solid #1d4ed8` instead of the invalid old `border: #1d4ed8` fallback, and missing tokens with authored fallbacks such as `var(--space, calc(1rem + 2px))` emit readable fallback CSS before preserving the authored declaration. The matrix/fixture row `custom-property-var-fallbacks` is now source-guarded beside the existing import, nesting, custom-media, selector, color, logical, and prefix canaries. Verification: red/green focused Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 13/13 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo/workspace builds, local servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, cascade-layer flattening, and universal Autoprefixer parity.

- Flow Forge standalone Cargo boundary, 2026-05-23: the root DX-WWW
  workspace now explicitly excludes `integrations/flow-forge`, keeping Forge
  as a standalone integration package instead of silently pulling its heavy
  media/VCS dependency graph into normal DX-WWW workspace checks. Added a tiny
  source guard so the boundary cannot disappear silently. Verification: red
  targeted Forge cargo test first failed on the workspace-boundary error, green
  Flow Forge boundary/schema Node guards, and green `cargo metadata
  --manifest-path integrations/flow-forge/Cargo.toml --no-deps
  --format-version 1`. The focused Forge integration test now progresses past
  workspace discovery but is blocked by a corrupted local Cargo registry
  extract for `cc-1.2.56` under `G:\Dev\Caches`; no global cache repair, broad
  build, server, or deploy was run.

- Dev overlay and favicon color-token cleanup, 2026-05-23: the DX hot-reload
  error overlay no longer injects hardcoded hex/RGB colors and instead uses
  semantic system color tokens for the source-owned diagnostics surface. The
  launch-template favicon SVG now avoids hardcoded black/white fills, uses
  `Canvas` / `CanvasText` / `currentColor`, and its Forge media status,
  restore receipt, package lock/status, read model, and cache-backed copy were
  refreshed to the new SHA-256. Verification: red/green hot-reload protocol
  guard, red/green launch-template source-honesty favicon guard, green
  media-restore guard, and green Forge package-lock check. Skipped by policy:
  local servers, browser visual proof, broad builds, deploys, and runtime HMR
  parity claims.

- Launch readiness naming cleanup, 2026-05-23:
  WebAssembly Bridge source generators now emit
  `wasm-bindgen-readiness-workflow` as the readiness surface id, the
  launch runtime and browser-preview runtime set
  `dxWasmLocalReadinessEnabled`, and public
  package docs now describe Data Fetching & Cache and Automation Connectors as
  readiness/workflow surfaces rather than showcase-only surfaces. Verification: red/green
  `launch-template-audit-cleanup`, focused WebAssembly Bridge source guards,
  and stale-marker scan.

- `dx build` route-handler source graph proof, 2026-05-23: `dx-www/src/build/source_engine` now discovers `app/**/route.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` and records source-owned route-handler evidence in the build manifest, build receipt summary, graph receipt, graph snapshot, readiness receipt, and Zed handoff. The installed smoke harness now fails if its tiny `app/api/health/route.ts` exists only in server contracts/deploy adapter output and not in `dx.www.sourceBuildManifest`. This is source graph/build proof only: live handler execution, helper-backed 501 conversion, installed-binary refresh, and external framework route-handler clone parity remain governed follow-ups. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`, green focused `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_records_app_api_route_handlers_in_manifest_and_graph --test source_build_engine -- --nocapture`, and targeted rustfmt check.

- Launch README truth and Automation Connectors receipt-marker cleanup, 2026-05-23:
  `README.md` now follows the current preview-manifest reality rather than the
  old 40/100 baseline: bounded Forge/template score 89/100, unbounded source score
  93/100, live-proof ceiling 89/100 until governed proof, 20 lock-backed lanes, 30 catalog source ids, and zero claimed live
  browser/provider packages. Automation Connectors now uses
  `data-dx-automation-local-receipt` across source, generated `/launch`, and
  the browser-preview fixture, without retaining the prior demo-named marker.
  Verification: red/green docs honesty guard, green
  focused Forge reality/score/audit cleanup/materializer/automations tests,
  `git diff --check`, and touched-file conflict scan. Skipped by policy:
  browser/runtime proof, provider credentials, broad builds, and deploys.

- Parser launch extension cleanup, 2026-05-23: `ComponentType::from_path` now
  classifies `.html` files as pages and leaves `.tsx` files as components,
  matching the parser module contract for launch authoring. The old `.pg` page
  branch is no longer an active parser path. Verification: red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt check. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo builds, local
  servers, browser automation, package installs, and deploys.

- Production asset legacy extension cleanup, 2026-05-23:
  `dx-www/src/production/mod.rs` no longer treats `.pg` / `.cp` as a distinct
  production template asset class. The legacy `BinaryTemplate` variant was
  removed because launch source should flow through `.html`, `.tsx`, CSS, JS,
  and static assets instead of old internal template extensions. Verification:
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt check. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo builds, local
  servers, browser automation, package installs, and deploys.

- Dev hot-reload legacy extension cleanup, 2026-05-23: `dx-www/src/dev/mod.rs`
  and `dx-www/src/dev/hot_reload.rs` now classify `.tsx` / `.html` as launch
  component changes while treating legacy `.pg` / `.cp` files as static assets.
  This removes a stale dev-facing path for forbidden template extensions without
  claiming external bundler HMR or removing the older internal parser constants.
  Verification: focused Node source guard and touched-file rustfmt passed;
  targeted Cargo unit proof timed out after 124s on the busy workspace without
  diagnostic output.

- Public schema/build-gate hardening, 2026-05-23: the copied n8n source manifest now uses stable schema `dx.integrations.n8n_nodes_base_reference` with numeric `format: 1` and no UTF-8 BOM, and the schema/status noise guard scans that manifest so public `.v1` suffixes do not quietly return. The DX Build readiness gate now rejects source-build receipts that contain any `node_modules` path even when `node_modules_required` is incorrectly false, writes exact path counts into the source projection, and keeps the gate split in small modules. Verification: red/green schema-noise guard and green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`. Skipped by policy: local servers, installed-binary refresh, deploys, package installs, and governed runtime proof.

- dx-style nested at-rule PostCSS nesting canary, 2026-05-23 RUN 22: `related-crates/style` now closes another real nesting gap without a JS PostCSS chain. Nested `@media` and `@supports` rules inside selectors are lowered by wrapping the expanded parent selector inside the at-rule, preserving nested child selectors and avoiding invalid `.card @media` / `.card @supports` output. The matrix/fixture row `nested-at-rule-transform` is now source-guarded beside the existing `@nest`, custom-media, strict range-media, logical, color, and prefix canaries. Verification: red/green focused Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 12/12 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt check. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo/workspace builds, local servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, cascade-layer flattening, and universal Autoprefixer parity.

- Launch public readiness-field cleanup, 2026-05-23: static `/launch` pages now
  use `auth-readiness-field` for form labels. This is a narrow public-surface
  naming cleanup only; package evidence selectors such as Zod, Scene, and WASM
  keep their existing names until the shared guards can migrate together.
  Verification: red/green `node --test
  benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts`.

- dx-style public compile boundary, 2026-05-23: `related-crates/style/src/core/mod.rs` now exposes the `engine` module as a real public API boundary for dx-check/Zed receipt contracts, instead of hiding exported receipt helpers behind a private module that trips `dead_code` under focused library checks. Added `benchmarks/dx-style-compile-boundary.test.mjs` to guard `#![deny(warnings)]`, `registry_snapshot_plan`, and the public `StyleEngine` / theme CSS re-exports. Verification: red/green Node source guard and green `cargo check --lib -p dx-style -j 1`. A quiet full-package check was not claimed because it was terminated without output while other cargo tests were active; broad builds, servers, and generated-app proof remain skipped.

- dx-style compound custom-media / place / image-set PostCSS canaries, 2026-05-23 RUN 21: `related-crates/style` now adds another generated-CSS proof slice for the Rust-native PostCSS replacement. Custom media tokens expand inside compound media queries such as `screen and (--wide)`, `place-items` / `place-content` / `place-self` emit align/justify fallbacks for legacy starter targets, and `image-set()` emits a `-webkit-image-set()` fallback declaration while preserving the authored declaration. The matrix/fixture rows `compound-custom-media`, `place-property-fallbacks`, and `image-set-prefix-fallback` are now source-guarded. Verification: red/green focused Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 11/11 tests passing, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo/workspace builds, local servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, universal image-set syntax normalization, and universal Autoprefixer parity.

- dx-style `@nest` / strict media / logical-directional PostCSS canaries, 2026-05-23 RUN 20: `related-crates/style` now adds another receipt-backed PostCSS replacement slice without a JS PostCSS dependency chain. The Rust-native compatibility path lowers safe `@nest .theme &` rules, normalizes strict media comparisons such as `(width > 48rem)` and `(height < 40rem)` to calc-based min/max queries, and emits LTR physical fallbacks for directional logical declarations including `margin-inline-start`, `padding-inline-end`, `border-inline-start`, `inset-inline-start`, and `text-align: start`. The matrix/fixture rows `nest-at-rule-transform`, `strict-media-range-syntax`, and `logical-directional-fallbacks` are now source-guarded. Verification: red/green focused Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 10/10 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, and green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence. Skipped by policy: broad Cargo/workspace builds, local servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, universal RTL/writing-mode fallback claims, and universal Autoprefixer parity.

- DX Build hub command action fix, 2026-05-23: readiness-gate required actions now derive the installed-smoke command from present receipt provenance. Hub-level gates that consume `www/.dx/receipts/build/latest.json` now emit `node www/tools/build/dx-build-installed-smoke.ts --json --receipt .dx/receipts/build/installed-binary-smoke-latest.json`, which is runnable from `G:\Dx`; www-local gates keep the shorter `node tools/build/...` shape. This keeps Friday, DX CLI, and Zed from displaying a command that only works inside `G:\Dx\www`. Verification: red/green focused readiness-gate test and live hub gate refresh.

- DX Build quality receipt expansion, 2026-05-23: readiness-gate `quality.files` now covers the installed-smoke entrypoint and all `tools/build/installed-smoke/` modules in addition to the readiness-gate modules. The live hub gate reports `smallModuleBoundary: true`, `entrypointUsesSplitModules: true`, and `maxLineCount: 165`, with installed-smoke `report.js` at 146 lines. This lets DX CLI, Friday, and Zed verify that the command they are asked to run is also maintained as split source-owned code. Verification: red/green readiness-gate quality test and live hub gate refresh.

- DX Build product-proof snapshot, 2026-05-23: readiness-gate reports and consumer snapshots now expose a compact `proofs` block for DX CLI, Friday, and Zed. It records `blockers`, `sourceReady`, `readinessProductReady`, `installedSmokePassed`, `installedSmokeBinaryRole`, `installedSmokeReadyForProduct`, `runtimeProofApproved`, and `productReady` without requiring consumers to infer production state from nested receipts. Live hub evidence is source `100/100`, product `82/100`, installed smoke `installed-default` but not passed, runtime proof not approved, and product proof still blocked. Verification: red/green readiness-gate product-proof test, full focused DX Build Node guard, and live hub gate refresh. Skipped by policy: release rebuild, binary promotion, full builds, servers, installs, deploys, and runtime proof.

- DX Build product-proof hardening, 2026-05-23: the readiness gate now consumes the governed `dx check launch --json` receipt as `receipts.checkLaunch`, and product readiness requires both an installed-default `dx.build.installedBinarySmoke` receipt and `launch_approved.approved === true`. `tools/build/dx-build-installed-smoke.ts` now records `binaryRole`, `binaryDefault`, and `binaryOverride`, so a candidate or debug binary can prove source behavior without being mistaken for installed release proof. Live evidence: default `G:\Dx\bin\dx-www.exe` is correctly labeled `installed-default` and still fails with 11 stale-binary blockers; candidate `G:\Dx\www\target\debug\dx-www.exe` passes the tiny TSX/CSS/assets fixture but is labeled `candidate-override`. Hub readiness remains source `100/100`, product `82/100`; hub `dx check launch --json` is present but blocked at `245/500`, and `www` launch check is blocked at `250/500` by missing governed runner/web/E2E/deploy evidence plus large-file debt. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts` and `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`; live installed smoke, candidate smoke, `dx check launch --json` from `G:\Dx\www`, and readiness-gate refresh were run. Skipped by policy: release rebuild, full Cargo/npm/bun builds, servers, installs, deploys, and binary promotion. Exact next action: governed release binary refresh, then installed-default smoke and approved launch proof.

- Lane 5 Forge package reality, 2026-05-23: **Payments**, **AI SDK**, and **Automation Connectors** are now real launch-template Forge packages rather than docs-only status rows. `payments/stripe-js`, `ai/vercel-ai`, and `automations/n8n` are present in `.dx/forge/source-manifest.json`, `.dx/forge/package-lock.json`, cache manifests, package-add receipts, safety archive receipts, `.dx/forge/package-status.json`, and `forge-package-status-read-model.ts`. The template exposes provider-boundary flows through Stripe checkout/webhook route handlers, AI App Router route handlers, and n8n connector readiness/local dry-run receipts without claiming live credentials or hosted execution. Scores moved to Payments `78`, AI SDK `77`, and Automation Connectors `76`; these are source-owned/package-reality scores, not live provider proof. Verification: red/green lane 5 package reality guard, green Forge package lock check, green shared Forge/template read-model guard, and green package source guard freshness checks for Payments, AI SDK, Automation Connectors, plus the refreshed 3D hash row that the shared guard surfaced. Exact next action: run provider smoke only with deliberate test credentials and record those receipts separately.

- dx-style touch-action edge utility parity, 2026-05-23 RUN 3: `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/utility/mod.rs` now emits normal CSS for Tailwind-familiar touch-action edge helpers without Tailwind/PostCSS: `touch-pan-left`, `touch-pan-right`, `touch-pan-up`, `touch-pan-down`, and `touch-pinch-zoom`. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt now records `touch-pan-left` and `touch-pinch-zoom` as supported interactivity fixtures, and the compatibility matrix/readme keep this scoped to measured subset support. - dx-style outline/ring edge utility parity, 2026-05-23 RUN 2: `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/utility/mod.rs` now emits normal CSS for Tailwind-familiar outline and ring edge helpers without Tailwind/PostCSS: `outline-2`, `outline-dashed`, `outline-dotted`, `outline-double`, `outline-hidden`, `ring-inset`, and `ring-offset-2`. Ring width utilities now include the `var(--tw-ring-inset,)` shadow slot so `ring-inset` can affect generated output. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt now records `outline-2`, `ring-inset`, and `ring-offset-2` as supported, and the compatibility matrix/readme keep this scoped to measured subset support. - DX Build source projection and Next/Rust merge guard, 2026-05-23: the readiness gate now has `--write-source-projection`, implemented in the small `tools/build/readiness-gate/projection.ts` module. It creates compact hub-level `dx.build.readiness` and `dx.build.zedHandoff` receipts from an existing real `dx.www.sourceBuildReceipt`, preserves Next/Rust quarantine evidence from `www/.dx/receipts/next-rust/vendor-boundary-consumer.json`, and keeps `product_ready: false` until installed-binary smoke and governed runtime proof pass. The gate report now publishes `receipts.nextRustBoundary`, blocks unsafe Next runtime/node_modules/full-parity claims if they appear, and stops asking Friday/Zed to refresh source receipts once source readiness and handoff pointers are valid. Hub projection now reports source `100/100`, product `82/100`, with blockers for unsealed product readiness and installed-binary smoke. Next/Rust merge safety was checked with the Lane 14 coordinator and audit-comparison guards, including the read-only `giant-cli-mod` split-risk check. - dx-style radial/conic generated-output receipt expansion, 2026-05-23: `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/parity.rs` now receipts the full already-supported radial/conic background canary set instead of only `bg-radial` and `bg-conic-180`: `bg-radial-[circle_at_center]`, `bg-radial-(--dx-bg-radial)`, `bg-conic`, `-bg-conic-45`, `bg-conic-[from_45deg_at_center]`, and `bg-conic-(--dx-bg-conic)` are now fixture-backed and asserted against real generated CSS. - Lane 5 CSS pipeline source-map source-role evidence, 2026-05-23 RUN 7: emitted `.css.map` evidence metadata now gives every `x_dx_css_pipeline.source_hashes[]` row a typed role: `entry-style` for the root stylesheet and `flattened-import` for local imports inlined by the source-owned CSS adapter. It still does not claim exact segment-level source maps, full LightningCSS parity, or Turbopack CSS as public architecture. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-css-pipeline-boundary.test.cjs`. Exact next action: in RUN 8 hardening mode, split/tighten Lane 5 source-map/read-model code and rerun the focused Cargo fixture after non-Lane compile blockers clear.

- Lane 5 CSS pipeline per-source source-map hash evidence, 2026-05-23 RUN 6: emitted `.css.map` evidence files now carry `x_dx_css_pipeline.source_hashes[]` with one DX-owned BLAKE3 hash per represented CSS source body. The source-build manifest records `source_map_source_hash_count`, the build receipt summary records `css_source_map_source_hashes`, graph style nodes and consumer snapshots expose the same count through `source_map_source_hash_count` and `sourceMapSourceHashCount`, and the Zed handoff mirrors `source_map_source_hash_count`. This gives DX/Zed/Studio stale-source evidence without claiming exact source-map segment mapping, full LightningCSS parity, or Turbopack CSS as public architecture. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-css-pipeline-boundary.test.cjs`. Exact next action: rerun the focused source-build Cargo fixture after the shared non-Lane compile blocker clears, then move to a generated-output dx-style compatibility canary.

- dx-style transform-gpu/cpu utility parity, 2026-05-23: `transform_utility` now emits normal CSS for Tailwind-familiar `transform-gpu` and `transform-cpu`, with `transform-gpu` composing `translate3d(var(--tw-translate-x, 0), var(--tw-translate-y, 0), 0)` plus the existing rotate/scale variables and `transform-cpu` sharing the default transform composition. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records `transform-gpu` as supported, `dx_style_support` treats `transform-*` utilities as generated instead of unsupported, and the compatibility matrix now names `transform-gpu`, `transform-cpu`, and `transform-none` explicitly. Verification: red `cargo test -q -p dx-style transform_gpu_generates_tailwind_familiar_css --test transform_css`, green `cargo test -q -p dx-style --test transform_css`, green `cargo test -q -p dx-style parity_receipt_records_supported_and_unsupported_classes --lib`, focused `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts` pattern checks for high-usage utility guards plus generated-output parity receipts, and `dx run --check .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts`. Exact next action: add the next generated-CSS/browser-compat canary, preferably a prefixed text-flow utility such as `hyphens-auto` or the next arbitrary `@supports`/`@container` nested-selector receipt case.

- Lane 5 CSS pipeline source-map integrity continuation, 2026-05-23 RUN 5: emitted `.css.map` sidecars now carry a receipt-visible BLAKE3 integrity hash. `write_css_source_map()` returns a typed output with path plus hash, `SourceBuildStyle` records `source_map_hash`, receipt summaries expose `css_source_map_hashes`, graph style nodes expose the hash, graph consumer snapshots expose `sourceMapHashCount`, and the Zed handoff exposes `source_map_hash_count`. This lets DX/Zed/Studio detect stale or mismatched map evidence without parsing map bodies or promoting Turbopack CSS to public architecture. - Lane 5 CSS pipeline source-map link continuation, 2026-05-23 RUN 4: emitted source-build CSS now appends a standard adjacent `/*# sourceMappingURL=... */` comment after dx-style-owned minify/prune/import handling, and the build manifest records `source_map_linked` for each style. Receipt summaries expose `css_source_map_links`, graph style nodes expose `source_map_linked`, graph consumer snapshots expose `sourceMapLinkCount`, and the Zed handoff exposes `source_map_link_count`. - dx-style nested arbitrary at-rule selector variant, 2026-05-23: the Rust state parser now accepts a safe Tailwind-adjacent arbitrary at-rule plus nested selector form such as `[@media_(any-hover:hover){&:hover}]:opacity-100`, splits the at-rule and selector through the existing wrapper/media machinery, and still fails closed for unsafe braces, empty selectors, unknown at-rules, quotes, semicolons, and un-nested selector fragments. The generated-output parity receipt now records this class as supported with real `StyleEngine` CSS, `dx_style_support` no longer reports it as unsupported, and the Tailwind compatibility matrix calls this nested-selector at-rule support a targeted subset rather than full arbitrary variant grammar. Verification: red/green `cargo test -q -p dx-style arbitrary_at_rule_selector_variant_generates_wrapped_css --test arbitrary_variant_css`, targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-style parity_receipt_records_supported_and_unsupported_classes --lib`, and focused `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts` pattern checks for arbitrary at-rule variants plus generated-output parity receipts. Exact next action: keep walking arbitrary variant grammar with another real generated-CSS canary, preferably nested `@supports`/`@container` selector forms or a browser-prefix receipt expansion.

This is a visible check-panel hydration step, not proof of live generated CSS parity or full Tailwind/PostCSS/Autoprefixer replacement. - Lane 5 CSS pipeline retained-import evidence, 2026-05-23 RUN 3: source-build CSS now records retained CSS imports with explicit DX-owned reasons instead of hiding them behind `import_count`. The gate discovers DX-WWW workspace readiness/Zed handoff receipts, the source-build receipt at `.dx/receipts/build/latest.json`, and hub-root installed-binary smoke, validates the canonical installed-smoke pointer contract, writes behind `--write` / `--write-snapshot`, reports small-module quality, and emits machine-readable `requiredActions` for DX CLI, DX-WWW, Zed preview, and Friday: `refresh-source-build-receipts`, `refresh-installed-binary-smoke`, and `run-governed-runtime-proof`. The installed `G:\Dx\bin\dx-www.exe` was smoke-checked without rebuilding and is confirmed stale relative to source: it still treats `build --help` as a real build, emits no `source_build_css_*` fields, and writes a Zed handoff without `style_optimization`. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-cli-help.test.ts`; skipped by policy: heavy rebuild, server, package install, deploy, governed binary refresh, live hydration proof, and broad runtime parity. Source status remains 100/100; product/runtime is capped at 82/100 until the governed binary refresh plus tiny fixture smoke proves the installed command.

- dx-style direct and conditional state alias coverage, 2026-05-22: `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/mod.rs` now registers common Tailwind state aliases for direct variants such as `target:*`, `read-only:*`, and `indeterminate:*`, while `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/states/mod.rs` shares the same aliases with `has-*`, `not-*`, and `in-*` selector composition. The covered aliases include even, visited, target, autofill, read-only/read-write, indeterminate, default, in-range, and out-of-range. This improves real generated CSS compatibility for everyday form/location/state selectors without claiming full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar or full Tailwind/PostCSS parity. Verification: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style has/not/in state aliases cover common Tailwind selectors"`, `dx run --check .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts`, and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-style` filters for `empty_engine_supports_common_form_and_location_state_variants`, `has_even_variant_maps_to_even_selector`, `not_visited_variant_maps_to_visited_selector`, and `in_read_only_variant_maps_to_read_only_ancestor_selector`. Skipped by policy: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks. Exact next action: add generated-output parity fixtures for these state aliases and publish their support/unsupported status into the style receipt.

- Source-build Zed handoff style summary, 2026-05-22: `dx.build.zedHandoff` now includes `style_optimization`, a compact manifest-derived summary of style node count, original/retained/pruned rule totals, and minified style count. This gives Zed preview, DX CLI/TUI, and DX-WWW the same style optimization truth already present in the compact graph snapshot without requiring a full graph walk. - Source-owned `dx build` receipt summary and TSX boolean attribute slice, 2026-05-22: `SourceBuildSummary` now exposes aggregate CSS optimizer totals (`css_original_rules`, `css_retained_rules`, `css_pruned_rules`, and `css_minified_styles`), and `cmd_build` mirrors them into the public build manifest as `source_build_css_*` fields. The TSX runtime transform now lowers boolean JSX attributes such as `<button disabled>` to `disabled: true` in source-owned browser ESM. This keeps DX CLI/TUI/Zed connected to the build engine through stable summary fields instead of requiring full manifest traversal. - Source-owned `dx build` ecosystem connection, 2026-05-22: the build engine now has a compiler-backed route shell plus a linked source-module graph, runtime transforms, local CSS import flattening, and CSS minify/prune metadata. `dx-www/src/build/source_engine/module_linker.rs` recursively links local TSX/JSX/TS/JS imports for each discovered App Router route, `module_runtime_transform.rs` turns safe standalone TS/JS helper exports, simple TSX leaf components, simple imported TSX component graphs, and default route component functions into browser ESM, and `module_linker_writer.rs` emits transformed `dxSourceText`, `dxRuntimeModule`, and `dxRuntimeExports` beside source metadata chunks. `css_imports.rs` flattens quoted local CSS imports before parsing so token CSS is emitted without raw local `@import` statements, while `css_usage.rs` and `css_optimizer.rs` emit minified CSS plus conservative prune counts. `route_output.rs` imports the entry chunk into the shell as `dxRouteEntryModule`, keeps `fullReactHydration: false` honest, and still writes fallback HTML, DX packets, page graphs, and route units through `dx_compiler::delivery::compile_react_app_route`. `ecosystem.rs` writes `.dx/receipts/build/latest.json`, `.dx/receipts/graph/latest.json`, `.dx/receipts/graph/consumer-snapshot.json`, and `.dx/receipts/build/zed-handoff.json`; the graph now includes `source-module-chunk` nodes, `imports-source-module` edges, `transform_kind`, `runtime_exports`, and style optimization fields for DX CLI/TUI and future Zed preview. Verification: red/green `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --test source_build_engine`, focused `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli module_runtime_transform --lib`, focused CSS optimizer tests, green Node graph consumer test, targeted rustfmt, source-level root `dx graph` smoke, and conflict-marker scan. Heavy builds, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, full Rolldown/Lightning CSS parity, installed-binary refresh, and live hydration proof remain deferred by policy. Checkpoint before the later CSS summary/graph/Zed handoff slices: 98/100 source-ready. Exact next action: run governed installed-binary/runtime smoke, then broaden TSX runtime lowering and CSS compatibility beyond the current source-owned fixture.

- TSX App Router request-prop, Link, and Image lowering, 2026-05-22: generic TSX App Router execution now passes matched route params and query search params into `build_tsx_source_render_surface`, exposes `data-dx-tsx-page-prop-bindings`, and publishes a `dx.tsx.requestPropBindings` contract in `__DX_TSX_SOURCE_RENDER__`. The source-owned renderer resolves safe `params.slug`, `params["slug"]`, `searchParams.query`, `searchParams["query"]`, simple destructuring aliases like `const { slug } = params`, renamed aliases like `const { slug: postSlug } = params`, and member aliases like `const preview = searchParams.query` into static DOM snapshots, layout/template composition, and source-owned component return previews when those values are already known from DX route matching. It also recognizes static-safe `next/link` and `next/image` imports, lowering `<Link href="/path">` to source-owned `<a>` output and `<Image src alt width height />` to source-owned `<img>` output with `data-dx-framework-component` markers, so common App Router navigation/media no longer look like unresolved components. This is a real App Router compatibility step without `node_modules`; it is not external framework runtime clone parity because async Server Components, arbitrary request code, cookies/headers, dynamic `generateMetadata`, rich Link prefetch semantics, Next Image optimization, and generic JavaScript execution remain blocked. Files changed: `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_execution.rs`, `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_execution/source_render.rs`, `benchmarks/tsx-app-router-page-props.test.ts`, `benchmarks/tsx-next-link-compat.test.ts`, `benchmarks/tsx-next-image-compat.test.ts`, `DX.md`, `TODO.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md`. Verification: red/green request-prop, Link, and Image Node guards, focused TSX/Next guard bundle `dx run --test benchmarks\tsx-next-image-compat.test.ts benchmarks\tsx-next-link-compat.test.ts benchmarks\tsx-app-router-page-props.test.ts benchmarks\nextjs-compatibility-map.test.ts`, `dx run --check` for touched TS guards, targeted `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check`, targeted `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www` with pre-existing unrelated warnings, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan. Broader `public-framework-contract` was attempted and currently fails outside this TSX slice because `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` no longer contains the expected `styles/globals.css` starter contract marker. Heavy checks skipped by policy: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and live browser parity proof. Exact next action: add a live/source fixture proving a dynamic `/blog/[slug]?preview=1` TSX route visibly renders route/search aliases, `next/link` anchors, and `next/image` media through the generic App Router shell.

- DX build graph / Turbopack source-study slice, 2026-05-22: implemented a source-owned `dx.build.graph` receipt writer under `tools/build-graph/` with split model/scanner/resolver/receipt/reader files and `docs/build-graph-model.md`. The slice models TSX routes, components, dx-style generated CSS, Forge package surfaces, dx-check receipts, public assets, and deploy outputs with `dx.build.graph`, `dx.www.moduleGraph`, and `dx.forge.sourceGraph` names. It uses Turbopack only as MIT-licensed upstream research/provenance from `vercel/next.js`; no upstream code was copied and DX does not publicly depend on Turbopack or Turborepo. - Next.js compatibility source-merge start, 2026-05-22: official `vercel/next.js` is already mirrored at `G:\WWW\inspirations\nextjs` with remote `https://github.com/vercel/next.js.git`, inspected commit `f3f56ecec2f3f8cefa0f0a1323ea406740251d5c`, and MIT license at `license.md`. Added `docs/NEXTJS_COMPATIBILITY_MAP.md` covering App Router, layouts, route handlers, metadata, server/client components, server actions, redirects/not-found, image/font/script boundaries, middleware, static export, and cache/revalidate, including what DX can reuse directly, what must stay an adapter boundary, and what is not useful because it depends on Node/package-manager assumptions. Implemented the first upstream-informed slice in `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_execution/next_navigation.rs`: `redirect()`, `permanentRedirect()`, and `notFound()` from `next/navigation` now produce `dx.next.appRouterControlFlow` metadata with `NEXT_REDIRECT` / `NEXT_HTTP_ERROR_FALLBACK;404` semantics, no `node_modules`, and safe head hints. Verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\nextjs-compatibility-map.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\nextjs-compatibility-map.test.ts`, and targeted `rustfmt`. Targeted Cargo is still blocked by unrelated dirty-tree missing modules under `dx-www/src/build/source_engine/{css,js_ts,receipt}.rs`; do not claim compile green until that lane restores those modules. Exact next action: add a source-owned runtime/browser proof that a TSX page using safe literal `redirect()` or `notFound()` emits the expected App Router metadata/head hints, then decide whether page rendering can propagate actual HTTP status safely.

- TSX context provider value seeding, 2026-05-22: generic App Router context metadata now carries safe literal `createContext(...)` defaults and safe literal `<Context.Provider value=...>` seeds into `context_runtime.initial_values`. The generated `__DX_CONTEXT_RUNTIME__` initializes named contexts from those values, App Router responses expose `data-dx-context-initial-values`, and the public framework guard checks `initial_values`, `provider_value_source`, `safe_literal_context_value`, and `context_initial_values` so this does not silently regress. This is still not full React context parity: dynamic provider expressions, provider tree reconciliation, `useContext` execution, concurrent rendering semantics, and arbitrary component/import execution remain unclaimed. Verification: `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, targeted `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check`, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www` passed with pre-existing Forge duplicate/dead-code warnings. Heavy checks skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad suites, and live React runtime proof. Exact next action: add browser QA for seeded context values, then lower deterministic provider trees only where the TSX renderer already composes the source tree safely.

- TSX context runtime, 2026-05-22: generic App Router responses now expose `data-dx-context-runtime` and inject a source-owned `__DX_CONTEXT_RUNTIME__` script backed by `dx.tsx.contextRuntime`. The runtime detects `createContext`, `.Provider`, and `useContext(...)` names, exposes `getContext`, `resolveContextValue`, `setContext`, and `attachContextRuntime`, reflects values to `data-dx-context-read` markers, and dispatches `dx:context-runtime-ready` plus `dx:context-value` without requiring `node_modules`. This is a real provider-value map for the supported TSX subset, not full React context parity: provider tree propagation, `useContext` execution, concurrent semantics, and component import execution remain blocked. Verification scope: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check` on touched TSX runtime files, and targeted `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www` with unrelated existing Forge warnings. Heavy/runtime checks skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad suites, and live provider propagation proof. Exact next action: add browser QA for `__DX_CONTEXT_RUNTIME__.setContext(...)`, then design provider-tree lowering only for source-owned components that the TSX renderer can compose deterministically.

- TSX effect scheduler runtime, 2026-05-22: the generated `dx.tsx.stateRuntime` now includes `dx.tsx.effectScheduler`, emits a runtime script even for effect-only routes, schedules effect records on mount and after compiler-owned dependency state changes, exposes `scheduleEffectsForState`, and dispatches `dx:effect-scheduled` plus `dx:effect-scheduler-flush` without requiring `node_modules`. - TSX class-list and class-call prop lowering, 2026-05-22: the source-owned App Router renderer now lowers bounded React class-list expressions such as `["button", active && "is-active", variant === "primary" && "button-primary"].filter(Boolean).join(" ")` plus `cn("button", active && "is-active")` / `clsx("button", variant === "primary" ? "is-primary" : "is-secondary")` when every item resolves from literals, caller prop bindings, template bindings, or static conditions. This improves normal shadcn-style `className` rendering for source-owned components without evaluating object/array `clsx` payloads, function calls, spreads, hooks, runtime data, or arbitrary JavaScript. Guarded with the public framework contract source guard; targeted Cargo remains blocked by unrelated shared package-lane churn while other workers are editing `dx_check_receipt.rs`. Exact next action: lower the next common TSX subset, safe event/form binding metadata for source-owned client islands, behind the same no-arbitrary-execution boundary.

- TSX static template and conditional prop lowering, 2026-05-22: the App Router source renderer now handles bounded static template literals and ternary branches with caller prop bindings in component return previews. Safe patterns such as `` `card ${className}`.trim() ``, `` `Hello ${props.name}` ``, `active ? "enabled" : "disabled"`, and `` variant === "primary" ? `button ${variant}` : "button" `` can lower into the static DOM preview when every interpolation/branch is a literal or a resolved prop alias. This is a real compatibility improvement for common className/text patterns in `.tsx`, but it is still not arbitrary JavaScript execution, hook execution, or full React runtime parity. Verification scope: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --tests`, and targeted helper unit tests with the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning. Heavy checks skipped: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and `just run`. Exact next action: add safe class-list lowering for `active && "class"` plus `.filter(Boolean).join(" ")`.

- Forge remote-head receipt consumption smoke, 2026-05-22: `dx forge status --json` now scans `.dx/forge/receipts/remotes` in addition to the older `.dx/receipts/forge` roots, and CLI remote-head reports prefer top-level `health_evaluation` before nested planned `remote_read_plan.object_head_health_evaluation`. This lets a measured future `dx forge remote-head --yes --approved-by ... --write-receipt` report flow into the same status/dx-check/Zed shape instead of being hidden behind the dry-run plan. Core dx-check JSON extraction now also recognizes top-level `health_evaluation`. The Forge benchmark adds a skip-safe local fixture smoke: publish a root-`dx` package to a local registry, use its manifest as `--remote-manifest`, run `dx forge remote-head auth/better-auth#client --write-receipt --json`, and then require `dx forge status --json` to see `remote_object_head_health` from the persisted receipt when the binary supports the new command. Verification scope: `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` (8 pass, 1 skip because the existing binary predates `remote-head`), and `cargo check -q -p dx-www-compiler --lib` passed with only the existing Three scene unused-constant warning. `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` is currently blocked by unrelated `app_router_execution/source_render.rs` API drift, and `rustfmt --check core\src\ecosystem\project_check.rs` reports unrelated formatting drift in other project-check lane modules. Heavy checks, live R2/S3 calls, full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, installs, deploys, and `just run` were skipped. Exact next action: rebuild or refresh the lightweight `dx-www` binary only in the governed check window, then run the remote-head smoke without the skip and wire the resulting status row into the visible launch-template/Zed panel.

The command requires a real `--remote-manifest <manifest.json>` fixture, builds the existing `dx.forge.remote_read_plan` + `dx.forge.remote_object_metadata_plan`, emits a `dx.forge.remote_head_cli_report`, and can persist `.dx/forge/receipts/remotes/<package>-<version>-r2-head-health.json` with `--write-receipt`. - Forge approval-gated R2/S3 HEAD adapter, 2026-05-22: Forge now has a small live-read boundary for approved R2/S3 object metadata checks in `core/src/ecosystem/forge_r2_head.rs`, exported through `core/src/ecosystem/mod.rs` and reusing the existing redacted `DxForgeR2Config` store builder without exposing secrets. The adapter requires an S3-compatible metadata plan, `network_allowed=true`, a non-empty approval operator, a provider-mode label, and `write_allowed=false`; it performs HEAD-only metadata reads, maps missing objects to `measured_exists=false`, records size/ETag/last-modified when available, and never writes, deletes, fetches blobs, installs packages, or prints secret values. Verification passed: `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check .\core\src\ecosystem\forge_r2_head.rs`, targeted Rust `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler live_r2_head_adapter_requires_explicit_network_approval --lib`, scoped `git diff --check`, and scoped whitespace/conflict scans. Heavy checks skipped: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live R2/S3 network calls, writes, and package install from remote. Exact next action: wire this adapter behind an explicit CLI/receipt command so approved operators can populate the existing remote HEAD health receipt from measured metadata.

- Add metadata so DX-WWW/CLI can discover launch templates and Forge package slices.

## Public TSX Framework Contract - 2026-05-22

- Public pitch: React-familiar apps with source-owned packages and no hidden dependency surface. Everything else should support that sentence, not compete with it.
- Public launch path is React-familiar `.tsx` with App Router-shaped routes. New public starter work should use `app/layout.tsx`, `app/page.tsx`, `app/dashboard/page.tsx`, `app/settings/page.tsx`, `app/auth/page.tsx`, and `app/billing/page.tsx`; legacy page formats are not the current public authoring path.
- The default launch template dashboard is the App Router page `app/page.tsx` at `/`; `app/page.tsx` remains the `/launch` alias for Studio/docs handoff. Package lanes register against `examples/template/template-surface-registry.ts` so auth, settings, billing, data, query, state, i18n, docs, motion, 3D, WASM, AI, automations, icons, and token/money meters plug into one source-owned shell instead of duplicating route-level proof sections.
- Latest Reactive Store package pass (2026-05-22): official `Reactive Store` package id `reactive/store` now materializes the selected upstream `react-context` surface in the launch template and writes `.dx/forge/receipts/packages/reactive-store.json` with SHA-256 file hashes. The package-status row has moved from `missing-receipt` to `present` for `lib/forge/state/reactive-store/context.tsx`, `metadata.ts`, and `README.md`, while still avoiding unused store, atom, selector, or comparison files. Upstream source re-inspected from `G:\WWW\inspirations\tanstack-store`: `packages/store/package.json`, `packages/react-store/package.json`, `packages/react-store/src/index.ts`, `packages/store/src/store.ts`, `packages/store/src/atom.ts`, `packages/store/src/shallow.ts`, `packages/react-store/src/useSelector.ts`, `packages/react-store/src/useAtom.ts`, `packages/react-store/src/useStore.ts`, and `packages/react-store/src/createStoreContext.tsx`; upstream APIs used remain `Store`, `ReadonlyStore`, `createStore`, `createAtom`, `createAsyncAtom`, `batch`, `flush`, `shallow`, `useSelector`, `useAtom`, `useStore`, and `createStoreContext`. Files changed: `benchmarks/reactive-store-package-status-read-model.test.ts`, `examples/template/lib/forge/state/reactive-store/context.tsx`, `examples/template/lib/forge/state/reactive-store/metadata.ts`, `examples/template/lib/forge/state/reactive-store/README.md`, `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/packages/reactive-store.json`, `examples/template/.dx/forge/package-status.json`, `examples/template/forge-package-status-read-model.ts`, `examples/template/package-catalog.ts`, `docs/packages/reactive-store.md`, `DX.md`, `TODO.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md`. Verification run: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\reactive-store-package-status-read-model.test.ts`, plus `dx run --test .\benchmarks\reactive-store-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-forge-package-status-read-model.test.ts`, `dx run --check` for both Reactive Store guards, scoped `git diff --check`, and scoped conflict-marker/trailing-whitespace scans. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, registry-wide rustfmt, and live runtime proof. Next action: teach shared dx-check output to evaluate the Reactive Store receipt hashes as `present` or `stale` directly instead of relying on the static package-status fixture.
- Public styling path is dx-style normal CSS: `styles/theme.dx.css` as the token source, `styles/app.generated.css` as generated CSS, and `dx style build|watch|check` as the front-door commands. `dx style check` now fails on missing required public tokens, stale generated CSS, unused generated classes, hardcoded colors, and Tailwind leakage. Core `dx check` also includes a `dx-style` section with the same token/generated-CSS/hardcoded-color/Tailwind-leakage signals so project checks catch styling drift even when the standalone style command is not run. Binary style output is not part of the launch pitch.
- dx-www style compiler now exposes a normal CSS renderer: `dx-www/src/build/style.rs` has `STYLE_PUBLIC_OUTPUT_MODE = "normal-generated-css"` and `StyleCompiler::compile_normal_css`, while DXC1 stays `deprecated-internal` compatibility instead of the public launch path.
- dx-www style parser now exposes TSX class attribute extraction: `dx-www/src/parser/style.rs` has `STYLE_CLASS_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` plus `extract_class_attribute_tokens` for static `className`/`class` strings, giving the PostCSS replacement path a Rust-owned scanner entry point before generator wiring.
- Static TSX expression class strings are now part of the scanner contract too: plain quoted attributes plus `className={\`...\`}`, `className={"..."}`, `className={'...'}`, and matching `class={...}` forms are recognized by the parser and `dx style` CLI scan path. This does not claim arbitrary JavaScript class expression parity.
- Static `classes()`, `dxClass()`, `cn()`, `cx()`, `clsx()`, `classNames()`, and `cva()` class strings are covered by the same scanner contract for common class composition and variant recipes. `classes()` is the preferred DX-owned helper for new source, while the other helper names stay supported for existing shadcn-style code. dx-style extracts quoted string tokens from those calls for normal CSS generation, but it does not claim arbitrary JavaScript parsing or runtime branch evaluation.
- Dynamic template class placeholders such as `dx-${variant}` are skipped instead of emitted as generated CSS selectors. The scanner keeps neighboring static tokens from the same attribute/helper call, but generated CSS remains limited to concrete class strings.
- `dx style` receipts now distinguish scanned static class tokens from generated style class tokens. dx style build now feeds scanned class tokens through the dx-style engine, so supported Tailwind-familiar utilities such as spacing, color, state, responsive, and container-query classes are emitted as normal CSS instead of placeholder selectors. Receipts split generated `dx-*` template classes from generated utility classes to keep unsupported tokens visible instead of pretending full Tailwind parity.
- launch runtime CSS bridge now aliases DX theme tokens (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--border`, `--muted`, `--success`, `--warning`, `--danger`) through `--dx-*` runtime variables instead of embedding hex/rgb color literals. Scene-generated TypeScript color helpers remain dynamic package data, but the CSS bridge is token-owned.
- DX-style compatibility status: current source score is 99/100 for the requested launch scope. The Rust crate is real and broad enough for the official DX-WWW starter path, but it does not have all Tailwind classnames. `related-crates/style/TAILWIND_COMPATIBILITY.md` is now the source of truth: layout/flex/grid/spacing/sizing/typography/colors/borders/radius/shadows/opacity/transforms/transitions/animation/filters/responsive/dark/state/data/aria/group/peer/arbitrary/negative/important/container-query support is a useful subset; full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar beyond guarded safe selector forms, custom Tailwind animation config/theme extension parity beyond guarded CSS variable aliases, transition plugin/theme extension parity, and Tailwind container-query config/edge grammar beyond the guarded min/max/named subset remain partial. Do not claim full Tailwind parity.
