- PostCSS replacement status: no root `postcss.config.*` or `tailwind.config.*` was found in the launch scan. The replacement is `dx style build|watch|check`: scan app/component/style files, read `styles/theme.dx.css`, treat `styles/globals.css` as an authored style entry, write normal `styles/app.generated.css`, and gate the generated CSS through receipts and `dx check`. `dx-www/src/cli/public_framework_tools.rs` now exposes a `postcss_replacement_contract` in style build/check receipts with scan roots `app`, `components`, `styles`, `forge`; extensions `tsx`, `jsx`, `ts`, `js`, `css`, `html`, `mdx`; class/className extraction patterns; no PostCSS adapter dependency; no Tailwind dependency; and no template-local `node_modules` requirement. The generated CSS also runs through the existing dx-style Lightning CSS parse/print compatibility pass and restores the generated source-hash header if the formatter drops comments, giving the public path a Rust-side CSS normalization step without adding PostCSS.
- `dx style check` now blocks legacy Tailwind/PostCSS config files at the project root (`postcss.config.*`, `tailwind.config.*`) through `legacy_css_tooling_findings`, so official DX-WWW starters cannot quietly drift back to a PostCSS/Tailwind pipeline while still claiming dx-style ownership.
- `dx style check` also blocks legacy Tailwind/PostCSS package dependencies in `package.json` (`tailwindcss`, `postcss`, `autoprefixer`, `@tailwindcss/postcss`) through `legacy_css_dependency_findings`, keeping the public template aligned with the no hidden dependency surface launch story.
- `dx style check` also scans common lockfiles for legacy Tailwind/PostCSS lockfile entries (`package-lock.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `yarn.lock`, `bun.lock`, `bun.lockb`) through `legacy_css_lockfile_findings`, so stale package-manager residue is visible even when `package.json` has already been cleaned.
- rkyv/memmap2 status for style: `memmap2` is actually used by `related-crates/style/src/cache/snapshot.rs`, `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/mod.rs`, and `related-crates/style/src/core/output.rs`; `rkyv` is not a dx-style crate dependency today, even though it appears elsewhere in the workspace lockfile.
- Registry/theme snapshot status: `related-crates/style/src/cache/registry_snapshot_plan.rs` now records the smallest isolated future snapshot lane for class registry keys, theme tokens, screen/container tokens, and checksums at `.dx/style/registry.snapshot`. It is a plan/contract only: normal generated CSS remains the public path, memmap2 is the intended read-only mapping layer, and rkyv remains unwired until a focused Rust compile/CLI smoke pass is approved.
- Public source hygiene now has `dx imports sync|check` for readable generated import maps, and `dx check web-perf` for Rust-owned web-performance receipts with Core Web Vitals, request/transfer evidence, and an attach-only Chrome DevTools Protocol collector plan. URL mode now writes `.dx/receipts/check/web-perf/cdp-plan.json` with device profiles, CDP domains/sequence, metric sources, score model, and explicit blocked-until conditions; exact Lighthouse parity can be imported through `--from-lighthouse report.json`, incomplete Lighthouse imports expose missing score categories instead of claiming a 400-point total, and URL/CDP mode still must not claim measured scores until governed runtime collection runs.
- Public deploy path is `dx deploy vercel --dry-run|--prebuilt|--prod`, now running a local preflight before it writes the static/prebuilt deploy manifest and receipt: dx-style build/check, import sync/check, `.dx/build` inspection, content-hashed upload planning, Vercel Build Output API v3 handoff metadata for `.vercel/output/static`, a `.dx/deploy/vercel-command.json` command contract, safe `.vercel/output` materialization for non-dry-run deploy preparation, and explicit external-deploy approval gating.
- Forge package claims use four public maturity labels: `slice`, `adapter-boundary`, `boundary`, and `full`. Current launch auth, payment, provider, hosted database, AI, InstantDB, and automations surfaces are adapter boundaries unless their runtime services are configured and tested by the app. That language is intentional: source-owned boundaries are valuable, but they are not full package replacements yet.
- Forge package status now has a receipt-backed DX-WWW read model: `examples/template/forge-package-lock.ts --check` validates `forge-package-status-read-model.ts` against `.dx/forge/package-status.json`, `.dx/forge/vcs-status.json`, `.dx/forge/remote-status.json`, `.dx/forge/media-status.json`, `.dx/forge/receipts/vcs/launch-template-snapshot.json`, `.dx/forge/receipts/remotes/launch-template-sync-plan.json`, and `.dx/forge/receipts/media/launch-template-favicon-restore.json`; `forge-package-status.ts` consumes that model for locked package rows, package-add receipts, cache manifests, rollback anchors, safety archive receipts, media cache/restore status, remote counts, remote sync-plan status, the 66-file VCS snapshot summary, dx-check metrics, and Zed receipt surface names. This proves the current three launch-owned slices, `shadcn/ui/button`, `state/zustand`, and `tanstack/query`, plus one cache-backed SVG media restore input, with 100% local rollback coverage and a safe multi-remote status receipt, without claiming a public registry, dependency solver, remote rollback, live network sync, full Git replacement, or broad Git LFS replacement.
- Framework completeness source of truth: `examples/template/framework-completeness.ts` now tracks the requested launch lanes for routing parity, server/client model, dev experience, production dashboard starter, and package ecosystem coverage. It is intentionally honest: TSX/App Router, dx-style CSS generation, auto imports, dx-check receipts, Forge packages, Studio markers, and dashboard workflows are marked as source-owned where they are real, while hosted auth/payment/database/AI/streaming surfaces stay adapter-boundary or partial until credentials and runtime proof exist. Generated starters materialize it as `components/launch/framework-completeness.ts`, and `dx check --project-contract` reports framework-completeness metrics.
- Receipt policy: receipts and provenance stay under `.dx/` and should be surfaced through `dx check`, Studio/Web Preview, and actionable error states. The happy path should remain `dx new`, `dx dev`, `dx check`, and `dx deploy`.
- DX-WWW can now consume the root `dx-check` command-center receipt directly: `read_dx_check_latest_panel` and `dx check --latest-receipt --json` read `.dx/receipts/check/check-latest.json`, validate the embedded `dx.check.zed_panel` payload, and return a compact `dx.www.check_panel` state for score meters, bucket breakdowns, blockers, warnings, quick fixes, and last-run metadata. The panel preserves `weight_profile: "dx-check.launch-default"` plus per-bucket weights so Web Preview and future Zed GPUI can render the 500-point model without guessing. It also carries `scoring_config` from `dx.check.scoring_config`, so panels can show default, detected-but-not-applied, or invalid future weight-config states without changing the active score. This is a read bridge only; it does not run Lighthouse, CDP, lint, format, or E2E execution by itself.
- Studio/Web Preview now has a source-owned dx-check panel contract: the preview manifest exposes `check_panel`, the launch shell/runtime page publish `data-dx-check-panel`, `data-dx-check-command`, `data-dx-check-receipt-path`, schema, score max, and Zed panel schema markers, and edit/materialization contracts map the panel back to source. This is still a render contract, not the final Zed GPUI implementation.
- The reader also returns `dx.www.check_panel_view_model`, a UI-ready shape with a score meter, weighted bucket rows, blockers, warnings, quick fixes, refresh/detail actions, last-run metadata, and explicit empty states for missing or malformed receipts. Missing/malformed states intentionally have no score meter.
- The root CLI source now owns shared `fixtures/dx-check` panel receipts for ready/warning, detected-but-not-applied scoring config, and unsupported-schema states, and DX-WWW reads those same fixtures in its parser tests so Studio/Web Preview and future Zed GPUI panels do not drift from the CLI contract.
- The root CLI also exposes `dx check fixtures --json` as a metadata-only fixture index for parser and GPUI consumers. It lists the shared fixture pack and launch-default weight profile without scanning projects, reading live receipts, running Lighthouse/CDP/E2E/lint/format, or writing `.dx/receipts/check`.
- The launch shell and runtime-safe `/launch.html` panel now consume the view-model shape directly: panel DOM markers expose schema, ready/missing/malformed status, score availability, bucket/finding/action counts, last-run state, and missing-receipt empty state without claiming a score before a receipt exists.
- Latest dx-check panel fixture pass (2026-05-22): added `panel-ready-detected-config.receipt.json` with `scoring_config.status = "detected_not_applied"`, `applies_to_score = false`, configured weights totaling 500, and a warning/quick-fix row that explains why the active launch score still uses `dx-check.launch-default`. Verification passed: fixture JSON parse, website `scripts/smoke-dx-check-receipt.mjs`, CLI `cargo test --test dx_check fixtures -- --nocapture`, and DX-WWW `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler dx_check_latest_panel -- --nocapture`. Skipped by instruction: servers, browser automation, package installs, full builds, broad suites, deploys, Lighthouse/CDP/E2E execution. Next action: use this same fixture pack in the first native Zed GPUI/live Studio panel renderer so scoring-config warnings are visible without inventing scores.
- Current architecture gap: generic TSX/App Router rendering is still the main engine risk. The launch responder is TSX-scoped and the generic fallback now rejects placeholder text, but full route composition, imports, props, client-boundary behavior, route handler parity, and common React hook compatibility remain open.
- Current TSX runtime step: non-launch App Router pages now go through `render_app_router_runtime` in `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_execution.rs` instead of hand-written metadata injection in the giant CLI file. The dev response advertises `data-dx-renderer="tsx-app-router-generic"` and embeds a `__DX_APP_ROUTER_EXECUTION__` JSON contract with route params, search params, layout/template/boundary counts, metadata sources, and no-`node_modules` status. This is a real framework contract, not full React execution parity yet.
- TSX semantics step: generic App Router execution contracts now include `tsx_semantics` from `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_semantics.rs` with schema `dx.tsx.appRouterSemantics`. The payload records layout/template/page composition, default exports, children slots, imports, source-owned versus external boundaries, hooks, event handlers, client/server boundaries, and warnings for dynamic imports, React context, and implicit client boundaries. This makes TSX more real without falsely claiming full React runtime parity.
- TSX source render/import step: generic App Router responses now expose `dx.tsx.sourceRenderSurface` through `__DX_TSX_SOURCE_RENDER__` and `data-dx-tsx-*` markers. The surface lowers route/segment TSX plus bounded source-owned local TSX/JSX imports into renderable elements, component references, prop bindings, and form surfaces. This improves Studio/check visibility for normal component imports without claiming full imported-component execution yet.
  - Lightweight verification: `rustfmt` for touched App Router execution files, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, and Cargo verification. Current broad Cargo verification is still blocked by unrelated dirty Forge registry work, so the next worker should keep this lane source-guarded until the registry lane is healthy.
  - Next action: lower the scanned source-owned component imports into an executable DOM snapshot/prop evaluator for the common literal-prop and child-composition subset.
- TSX static DOM snapshot step: the same source render surface now carries `dx.tsx.staticDomSnapshot`, a safe common-subset HTML snapshot built from intrinsic JSX elements in the route, segment files, and bounded source-owned imports. Runtime markers now expose `data-dx-tsx-static-snapshot` and `data-dx-tsx-static-snapshot-elements` so Studio/check consumers can see whether a route has a concrete static DOM skeleton. This still does not execute component functions, hooks, arbitrary expressions, or event handlers.
  - Lightweight verification: `rustfmt` for touched App Router execution files, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
  - Next action: attach the static snapshot to literal prop evaluation, child composition, and state/event slot binding so ordinary local components become executable without pretending to be full React.
- TSX literal prop evaluation step: the static snapshot now preserves safe literal expression props and direct child expressions, including quoted strings, static template literals without interpolation, numbers, booleans, and nullish values. The source surface reports `static_dom_snapshot_literal_expressions`, and the DOM exposes `data-dx-tsx-literal-expressions` so Studio/check can see how much of a route was lowered through the safe literal evaluator. Identifiers, function calls, object/array expressions, spreads, ternaries, maps, hooks, and runtime data reads remain skipped.
  - Lightweight verification: `rustfmt` for touched App Router execution files, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
  - Next action: bind literal-aware snapshot nodes to child composition and state/event slot attachment for source-owned local components.
- TSX component invocation input step: source-owned component composition entries now include `dx.tsx.componentInvocationInputs`, carrying literal/static props, event-handler boundaries, spread prop boundaries, direct text children, direct literal child expressions, skipped child expressions, and static-renderer eligibility. This is the next common-subset bridge toward executing local components, but it still does not run component functions, bind `children` into returned JSX, attach events, run hooks, or claim React parity.
  - Lightweight verification: `rustfmt` for touched App Router execution files, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
  - Next action: use the invocation input contract to render source-owned local component return skeletons with `children` insertion and state/event slot attachment.
- TSX children insertion preview step: source-owned component composition entries now include `dx.tsx.componentReturnPreview`, a bounded return preview that serializes safe intrinsic JSX from the imported component file and substitutes direct `{children}` / `{props.children}` placeholders with caller direct text and literal child expressions when possible. Nested child JSX ordering, prop identifier evaluation, component function execution, event attachment, hooks, and context remain out of scope.
  - Lightweight verification: `rustfmt` for touched App Router execution files, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
  - Next action: connect the children-aware return preview to state/event slots and intrinsic form lowering for a genuinely executable common local-component subset.
- TSX component runtime binding plan step: source-owned component return previews now carry `dx.tsx.componentRuntimeBindingPlan`, matching each local component source file to state slots, event slots, and intrinsic form-lowering metadata while keeping `full_dom_binding: false` and React submit handler execution unclaimed.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, touched-file `rustfmt`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
- TSX DOM action descriptor step: component runtime binding plans now carry `dx.tsx.domActionDescriptors` for safe intrinsic `button`, `input`/`textarea`/`select`, and `form` controls. The descriptors preserve React-style event attributes, matching state-event slots, input/control metadata, and native submit boundaries while explicitly keeping `browser_listeners_attached: false` and `full_react_event_execution: false`.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, touched-file `rustfmt`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
- TSX DOM action binder step: generic App Router responses now aggregate safe DOM action descriptors into `dx.tsx.domActionBinder`, expose `data-dx-dom-action-binder` / `data-dx-dom-action-descriptors`, and inject a no-`node_modules` `__DX_DOM_ACTION_BINDER__` script. The script binds safe `click`, `input`/`change`, and native `submit` preview listeners, dispatches `dx:dom-action-preview`, and announces `dx:dom-action-binder-ready` without running React handler bodies or React synthetic events.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, touched-file `rustfmt`, `git diff --check`, and a touched-file conflict-marker scan.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
- TSX DOM action state-runtime bridge step: the DOM action binder now exposes `dx.tsx.domActionStateBridge`, dispatches matched safe action previews to `window.__DX_STATE_GRAPH_RUNTIME__.dispatch`, and emits `dx:state-runtime-dispatch`. The generated state runtime now applies the lowerable common `useState` operations it already inferred: `set-from-input`, `toggle`, `add`, `subtract`, and `set-literal`.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, and touched-file `rustfmt`.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, and Cargo verification.
- TSX state DOM reflection step: generic App Router source render now emits `dx.tsx.stateDomReflection` for direct state reads such as `{count}`, `value={name}`, `checked={open}`, `disabled={busy}`, and `aria-expanded={open}`. The static DOM snapshot carries compiler-owned `data-dx-state-*` markers, and the generated state runtime has a `reflectStateSlotToDom` hook that updates only those marked text/control/boolean/aria targets while keeping `full_react_reconciliation: false`.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, scoped `rustfmt`, `git diff --check`, and touched-file conflict-marker scans.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, Cargo verification, and live React/browser QA.
- TSX served state-preview step: generic App Router responses now insert the safe static DOM snapshot as a hidden `data-dx-tsx-static-dom-preview="safe-common-subset"` surface before runtime scripts. That gives the generated state runtime and DX Studio a real response-DOM target for `data-dx-state-*` markers, while the UI remains honest that this is a static preview surface, not full React reconciliation.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, scoped `rustfmt`, and touched-file source inspection.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, Cargo verification, and live React/browser QA.
- TSX simple prop identifier step: source-owned component return previews now include `dx.tsx.componentPropIdentifierBindings`. The preview lowers literal caller props into `{propName}` and `{props.propName}` reads in safe intrinsic preview HTML, counts skipped dynamic/spread/event props, and keeps `full_prop_evaluation: false`.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `dx run --check benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, scoped `rustfmt`, and touched-file source inspection.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, Cargo verification, and live React/browser QA.
- TSX destructured prop alias step: source-owned component return previews now include `dx.tsx.componentDestructuredPropAliases`. The preview scans source-owned component files for simple object-pattern parameters such as `function Card({ title })` and `const Card = ({ title }) => ...`, records supported `function-parameter-object-pattern` and `arrow-parameter-object-pattern` signatures, and uses those aliases to resolve literal caller props in safe intrinsic preview HTML.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, scoped `rustfmt`, and source inspection of the bounded alias scanner.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, Cargo verification, and live React/browser QA.
- TSX renamed/default prop alias step: the same destructured-prop contract now records `renamed-alias-object-pattern` and `default-value-object-pattern`. DX-WWW can resolve simple `{ title: heading }` aliases and static literal defaults such as `{ title = "Untitled" }` in safe intrinsic return previews, still without executing component functions or arbitrary parameter logic.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, scoped `rustfmt`, and source inspection of the alias/default resolver.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full builds, broad test suites, package installs, deploys, Cargo verification, and live React/browser QA.
- TSX parser backend hardening: `core/src/delivery/tsx_ast.rs` now exposes `dx.tsx.parserBackend`, validates TSX syntax with `oxc_parser::Parser` whenever the `oxc` feature is enabled, and derives import declarations plus `export const metadata` route metadata from the Oxc `Program` AST on the public CLI path. `DxTsxModuleAst`, `DxReactJsxDocument`, and the generic App Router source-render surface now carry parser backend status, and the public `dx-www` CLI feature enables `dx-www-compiler/oxc` so this is on the public TSX path instead of being an unused optional crate.
  - Current honest limit: Oxc now owns TSX syntax validation, import declaration extraction, route metadata extraction, and JSX element/attribute/text/expression traversal for common React-shaped expression surfaces. Component function execution, full prop evaluation, hook semantics, effect ordering, context, and real client-island reconciliation are still not claimed.
  - Lightweight verification: red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, scoped `rustfmt`, `cargo check -q -p dx-www-compiler`, `cargo check -q -p dx-www-compiler --features oxc`, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli`.
  - Skipped: local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, full workspace builds, full test suites, and live React/browser QA.
- Current route-handler step: dev routing now resolves dynamic `app/api/**/[segment]/route.ts` paths and the safe interpreter supports `export const METHOD = async (...) => { ... }` handlers. Server contracts now capture exported-const request signatures such as `(request: Request)` so Studio/check output can distinguish web-request route handlers from generic JSON boundaries. Factory-based const handlers are detected as real exports and return a clear `route-handler-boundary` response until a dedicated Forge interpreter exists, which is more honest than claiming the method is missing.
- Request-context fix: route/static lookup still strips query strings for cache-busted assets, but App Router matching receives the original request path. Dynamic routes now keep `searchParams` in the runtime contract and DOM markers without regressing `/?qa=1` and `/styles/file.css?v=123` normalization.
- CLI structure step: API route discovery now lives in `dx-www/src/cli/app_api_routes.rs`, and App Router page-route matching now lives in `dx-www/src/cli/app_page_routes.rs`. This does not solve the whole `mod.rs` size problem, but it is the intended direction: small route/dev/template/Forge modules with the CLI acting as a router.
- Developer/AI understanding step: `docs/DX_WWW_MANAGER_HANDOFF.md` records the manager handoff, and the CLI now has source-only `dx explain /route`, `dx doctor`, `dx check packages`, and `dx export --analyze` surfaces. These write `.dx/routes/*.json`, `.dx/receipts/doctor/report.json`, `.dx/receipts/check/packages.json`, and `.dx/receipts/export/analyze.json` so humans, AI, and Zed can inspect route meaning, package maturity, dependency-black-hole risk, and static export size without a server run.
- Risk-register guard: `dx doctor` now emits `framework_risks` with schema `dx.framework.riskRegister` and a `score_ceiling`. Known risks such as incomplete generic TSX runtime parity, the oversized CLI module, Forge package overclaiming, missing static/export/web-performance proof, missing Studio manifests, public-story crowding, and source-copying/legal risk now cap the doctor score instead of allowing a fake 100/100.

## Forge Public CLI Surface - 2026-05-22

- Public Forge commands now have launch-shaped aliases in DX-WWW: `dx forge publish --registry local`, `dx forge publish --registry r2 --package <id> --dry-run`, `dx forge status --json`, `dx forge remotes --json`, and `dx forge receipts --json`.
- `dx forge status --json` now reports the one-file root package state and matching local-registry package state directly and writes the canonical latest receipt at `.dx/receipts/forge/status-latest.json` for DX CLI/TUI and Zed consumers. The JSON includes `root_package`, `local_registry_package`, and `receipt_path` with package id, version, export counts, default exports, selective-import readiness, manifest path, and verified/missing/invalid status without running a server, doing cloud work, or reading secrets.
- Selective imports are wired for current surface-package families: `dx forge add shadcn/ui#button,card,input` and `dx forge add ui --only button,input` expand to real package ids and still use the existing front-facing install planner.
- Generic root `dx` package export maps now feed the same planner: `[package]`, `[forge]`, `[[forge.files]]`, `[[forge.exports]]`, and `[forge.install]` can make `dx forge add auth/better-auth#client --dry-run` plan only the selected front-facing files without creating `node_modules`.
- Root `dx` package publish is now real for the local filesystem registry: `dx forge publish --registry local --package <id>` writes `.dx/forge/registry/local/index.json`, the package manifest, content-addressed file blobs, export/default-export metadata, and a local publish receipt.
- Local registry install is now real for root `dx` packages: `dx forge add auth/better-auth#client --registry local --dry-run` loads the verified local registry package, selects export files, and feeds the existing no-overwrite front-facing install planner.
- Local registry update is now real for tracked Forge packages: `dx forge update auth/better-auth#client --registry local --dry-run|--write` resolves a verified local registry version, honors selected exports, compares against `.dx/forge/source-manifest.json`, writes only green updates, and records `selected_exports` in the update receipt. Selective root `dx` update without `--registry local` is intentionally blocked because Forge needs a published export map to avoid guessing.
- Latest local update proof (2026-05-22): `forge_local_registry_update_selected_export_e2e_from_root_dx_versions` now locks the v1-to-v2 one-file `dx` flow at source level: publish v1 to `.dx/forge/registry/local`, install only `client`, publish v2, plan/write the green local-registry update, verify the update receipt keeps `selected_exports=["client"]`, and verify the unselected `server` file plus `node_modules` are not materialized. `benchmarks/forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` now also performs skip-safe existing-binary CLI smokes when `target/debug/dx-www` is already present: local publish/add/update for `auth/better-auth#client`, plus root `dx` R2/S3 dry-run publish with fake Cloudflare/S3-compatible env values. The R2 smoke verifies redacted configured status, planned `index.json`, package manifest, content-addressed file, and receipt object keys, and asserts fake account/access/secret values plus secret env marker names do not appear in JSON output. Lightweight verification passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`; Cargo build/test, servers, npm/bun/cargo installs, browser automation, deploys, rebuilds, and cloud publish were skipped by instruction. Latest status JSON proof passed with `cargo test -q -p dx-www forge_public_status_reports_root_dx_package_and_local_registry_export_readiness`, covering root package/local registry export readiness. Next action: add the same existing-binary R2 dry-run smoke for missing or partial credentials so DX CLI/TUI/Zed can distinguish safe unconfigured remotes from configured dry-runs without leaking values.
- Latest R2 readiness pass (2026-05-22): `DxForgeR2Status` now exposes redacted `access_key_id_set` and `secret_access_key_set` booleans alongside `account_id_set`, bucket, endpoint, public base URL, and prefix. `dx forge status/remotes --json` public remote state maps those booleans so DX-WWW, dx-check, and future Zed panels can distinguish configured, missing, and partial R2/S3 credential setup without printing credential values. Lightweight verification passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`; Cargo build/test, binary rebuild, servers, npm/bun/cargo installs, browser automation, deploys, and cloud publish were skipped by instruction. Follow-up completed below: missing/partial R2 smoke now covers remotes and publish dry-run output.
- Latest R2 actionability pass (2026-05-22): Forge public remote JSON now adds `setup_status` and redacted `missing_config` labels for local and R2 remotes. R2 status can now be `configured`, `partial-config`, or `missing-config`, with missing labels such as `account_id_or_endpoint`, `access_key_id`, `secret_access_key`, and `bucket`; this gives DX-WWW, dx-check, and future Zed GPUI panels a stable display contract without secret values or live cloud calls. Lightweight verification passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`; Cargo build/test, binary rebuild, servers, npm/bun/cargo installs, browser automation, deploys, and cloud publish were skipped by instruction. Follow-up completed below: core publish dry-run `r2_status` now carries the same labels.
- Latest R2 next-action and value-redaction pass (2026-05-22): Forge public `status` and `remotes` JSON now derive `next_actions` from the same redacted remote state. Missing or partial R2 setup now produces guidance that names only safe labels from `missing_config`, configured R2 points users to the dry-run publish path before any cloud write, and local registry absence still points to the safe local publish command. Low-level R2 dry-run status now serializes bucket/endpoint/public-base-url readiness booleans instead of values, and dry-run object plans use `<configured-bucket>` / `<unconfigured-bucket>` labels. Lightweight verification passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`; Cargo build/test, binary rebuild, servers, npm/bun/cargo installs, browser automation, deploys, and cloud publish were skipped by instruction. Next action: after a governed binary refresh, run hard existing-binary assertions for `dx forge remotes --json` and R2 dry-run `setup_status`, redacted booleans, and redacted object plan labels.
- Latest R2 dry-run status pass (2026-05-22): core `DxForgeR2Status` now carries `setup_status` and redacted `missing_config` directly, so `dx forge publish --registry r2 --package <id> --dry-run --format json` returns the same `configured` / `partial-config` / `missing-config` labels as `dx forge remotes --json`. The existing-binary smoke now proves missing and partial R2 setup for both remotes and publish dry-run output without leaking account/access/secret env values. Lightweight verification passed with `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_config`, `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`; full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, and cloud publish were skipped. Next action: add remote install/update/uninstall planning or a governed live R2 proof only after explicit approval.
- Latest R2 remote lifecycle pass (2026-05-22): `dx forge add/update/remove --registry r2` now exposes a dry-run-only `dx.forge.remote_lifecycle_plan` boundary with action, package id, selected exports, requested version, R2 setup labels, object-key plan, receipt plan, and `write_allowed: false`. The commands do not perform network reads, writes, deletes, sync, or live cloud cleanup; they are operator/Zed planning contracts until a real provider adapter is approved. This pass also keeps the old selected-export terminal banner out of R2 JSON output so `--format json` remains consumable by DX-WWW, dx-check, and future Zed GPUI. Existing-binary smoke coverage now runs `dx forge add/update/remove auth/better-auth#client --registry r2 --dry-run --format json`, proves the lifecycle schema, selected exports, requested versions, no materialized package files, no `node_modules`, and no leaked R2 env/config values. Latest lightweight verification passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`; full builds, broad Cargo suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, and live cloud work were skipped. Next action: add the real R2 provider adapter interface for remote manifest fetch/read-only install planning, still dry-run-only and gated from writes.
- Latest R2 read-only provider contract pass (2026-05-22): core Forge now exposes `DxForgeRemoteProviderKind`, `DxForgeRemoteReadIntent`, `DxForgeRemoteReadObject`, `DxForgeRemoteReadPlan`, and `plan_r2_remote_read_only_install`, and the public R2 add/update/remove dry-run source carries `remote_read_plan` with `schema_version = dx.forge.remote_read_plan`. The plan names the future read targets for `latest.json`, package `manifest.json`, and `files/<content-hash>` while keeping `network_allowed: false` and `write_allowed: false`, so DX-WWW, dx-check, and future Zed can display the provider boundary without implying live remote install works. Verification passed with the red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_read_only_plan_keeps_network_and_writes_disabled`, touched-file `rustfmt --check`, and scoped `git diff --check`; full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, and cloud publish were skipped. Next action: feed a real manifest fixture/read model into the read-only R2 plan so remote install dry-runs can compute front-facing conflicts without network or writes.
- Latest R2 manifest-fixture preview pass (2026-05-22): `plan_r2_remote_read_only_install_from_manifest_fixture` parses a real registry package manifest fixture, verifies package id/version and manifest integrity, resolves selected exports, maps files into front-facing project paths, hashes existing target files, and emits `dx.forge.remote_manifest_install_preview` with matching/missing/conflicting counts while `network_allowed=false` and `write_allowed=false`. The public CLI now accepts `dx forge add package#surface --registry r2 --dry-run --remote-manifest <manifest.json> --format json` for fixture-backed dry-runs only; this still does not fetch R2 objects, copy blobs, or perform remote install/update/remove. Verification passed with the red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_manifest_fixture_preview_reports_front_facing_conflicts`, and touched-file `rustfmt --check`. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, binary rebuilds, and live cloud publish. Next action: add a real provider trait that can perform approval-gated read-only manifest/object metadata checks against R2/S3 without writes or secret leakage.
- Latest R2 object-metadata planning pass (2026-05-22): Forge now has a typed `DxForgeRemoteReadProvider` boundary plus `DxForgeR2ReadOnlyProvider`, `DxForgeRemoteObjectMetadataPlan`, and `DxForgeRemoteObjectMetadataCheck` so fixture-backed remote plans include exact provider metadata checks for `latest.json`, package `manifest.json`, and each content-addressed blob. Every check is `metadata_operation = head-object`, carries expected hash/bytes for content blobs, and is marked `planned-not-checked` with `network_allowed=false` and `write_allowed=false`; this is still not a live R2 HEAD/GET implementation. Verification passed with the red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_manifest_fixture_plans_object_metadata_without_network`, and the existing source guards. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, binary rebuilds, live cloud publish, and live R2 metadata requests. Next action: add an approval-gated provider execution receipt shape for read-only HEAD checks so a future live probe can record measured object existence without enabling install/write/delete.
  - Final lightweight verification also passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, full targeted `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_manifest`, `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check core\src\ecosystem\forge_registry.rs`, scoped `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace scan, and touched-file conflict-marker scan. A wider `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check dx-www\src\cli\mod.rs` still reports existing import-order diffs in App Router/public-framework CLI files outside this Forge slice; this pass did not reformat those unrelated files.
- Latest R2 HEAD receipt-shape pass (2026-05-22): fixture-backed remote read plans now attach `dx.forge.remote_object_head_execution_receipt` with the exact planned `head-object` entries, manifest expected hashes/byte lengths, explicit `approval_required=true`, `approved=false`, `executed=false`, and empty measured remote metadata fields. The receipt keeps `dry_run=true`, `network_allowed=false`, and `write_allowed=false`, so DX-WWW, dx-check, and future Zed can render the future live-probe evidence contract without claiming Forge performed any R2/S3 request. Verification passed with the red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_head_execution_receipt_requires_approval_without_network`. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, binary rebuilds, live cloud publish, and live R2 HEAD/GET requests. Next action: add an approval-gated provider-execution harness that populates the receipt from a fake/test HEAD provider before any real R2 HEAD implementation.
  - Final lightweight verification also passed with `dx run --check .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, full targeted `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_manifest`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_head_execution_receipt_requires_approval_without_network`, `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check core\src\ecosystem\forge_registry.rs`, scoped `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace scan, and touched-file conflict-marker scan.
- Latest R2 fake-provider HEAD execution pass (2026-05-22): Forge now exposes `DxForgeRemoteObjectHeadProvider`, `DxForgeRemoteObjectHeadMeasurement`, `DxForgeRemoteObjectHeadExecutionApproval`, and `execute_r2_remote_object_head_checks_with_provider` so an approved injected provider can populate the same `dx.forge.remote_object_head_execution_receipt` with measured object existence, byte length, ETag, and last-modified values. The only proven execution path is a fake/test provider with `network_allowed=false`, so this is still not live R2/S3 probing; it is the boring harness needed before a real adapter can be allowed. Verification passed with the red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_head_execution_harness_populates_fake_provider_measurements`. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, binary rebuilds, live cloud publish, and live R2 HEAD/GET requests. Next action: add missing-object and byte-mismatch evaluation on the measured receipt so dx-check/Zed can flag unsafe remote state before any install.
- Latest R2 HEAD health evaluation repair (2026-05-22): restored the declared `core/src/ecosystem/forge_remote_health.rs` module as a real receipt evaluator instead of leaving the workspace with a missing module. It turns `dx.forge.remote_object_head_execution_receipt` into `dx.forge.remote_object_head_health`, classifies not-measured, healthy, missing required, missing optional, and byte-mismatched objects, and returns `safe_for_remote_install`, blocking counts, warnings, and next actions. This repair was needed before the focused dx-style Rust guard could compile. Verification passed: `cargo test -q -p dx-www unsupported_scan_report_only_flags_tailwind_like_utility_tokens --lib` and `rustfmt --check .\core\src\ecosystem\forge_remote_health.rs`. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, binary rebuilds, live cloud publish, and live R2 HEAD/GET requests. Next action: wire the remote health evaluation into dx-check/Zed/Forge status receipts so measured remote object failures become visible without running a broad suite.
- Latest R2 HEAD health read-plan pass (2026-05-22): `DxForgeRemoteReadPlan` now serializes `object_head_health_evaluation` beside `object_metadata_plan` and `object_head_execution_receipt` for fixture-backed R2 manifest previews. Planned receipts now expose required not-measured objects as blocking, and measured fake-provider receipts distinguish optional missing `latest.json` from byte-mismatched content blobs. This is consumer-ready health interpretation for dx-check, DX-WWW, and future Zed, but still not live R2/S3 probing or remote install execution. Verification passed with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` and `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler r2_remote_head_execution`. Skipped by instruction: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, browser automation, binary rebuilds, live cloud publish, and live R2 HEAD/GET requests. Next action: consume `object_head_health_evaluation` from `dx forge status`, dx-check remote-health scoring, and the future Zed panel before any live R2 adapter work.
- Latest R2 HEAD health status/check pass (2026-05-22): `dx forge status` now consumes saved remote lifecycle/status receipts with `object_head_health_evaluation`, publishes compact `remote_object_head_health` rows in `dx.forge.status`, and changes the status to `remote-health-blocked` when blocking remote object checks exist. Core dx-check reads `.dx/receipts/forge/status-latest.json`, exposes `forge_remote_head_health_receipts`, safe receipt counts, blocking check counts, missing required/optional counts, and byte mismatch counts, and raises `forge-remote-head-health-blocked` as a high-severity finding. This is still receipt/status consumption, not live R2/S3 execution. Verification passed with the Forge source guard, the existing-binary smoke guard, and focused `dx-www-compiler` dx-check test; the targeted `dx-www` CLI compile test timed out and was not retried because of the shared-machine policy. Next action: render `remote_object_head_health` in the launch-template/Zed status panel, then wire an approval-gated live HEAD adapter.
- Latest launch-template R2 HEAD health surface pass (2026-05-22): the launch-template package-status maintainer now writes and checks `.dx/forge/receipts/remotes/launch-template-r2-head-health.json` with schema `dx.forge.remote_object_head_health`, and `.dx/forge/package-status.json`, `forge-package-status-read-model.ts`, `forge-package-status.ts`, and `launch-route-contract.ts` expose `remote_object_head_health` / `remoteObjectHeadHealth`, Zed surface `remote-object-head-health`, and the `forge_remote_head_health_*` dx-check metrics. The receipt intentionally reports `safe_for_remote_install=false` because required R2/S3 HEAD checks are not measured; this is a visible remote-install blocker, not live cloud proof. Verification passed with targeted Node syntax/status/read-model guards plus scoped diff/conflict checks; heavy builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and live R2/S3 calls were skipped. Next action: implement an approval-gated live R2/S3 HEAD adapter that populates the same receipt with measured object metadata without enabling writes.
- Local package removal is now archive-backed for tracked Forge packages: `dx forge remove <package> --dry-run|--write` reads `.dx/forge/source-manifest.json`, hashes tracked files, refuses locally edited files, archives clean files under `.dx/forge/archive/...` before deletion, removes the package from the manifest, and writes a remove receipt. This is local/front-facing package lifecycle proof, not remote uninstall.
- R2/S3 remains dry-run-first. Root `dx` package R2 publish currently produces a redacted object-key plan only; env readiness now recognizes the launch fallbacks (`CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`, `DX_FORGE_R2_BUCKET`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, and `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL`) without printing values; public and low-level live registry publish paths require explicit write confirmation, and root-package live R2 upload is still blocked boundary work.
- Local registry publish is still also real for the built-in Forge launch catalog through `.dx/forge/registry/local`. Package update is green-only for curated/latest and local-registry packages. R2 remote install/update/uninstall now has dry-run planning only; live R2 proof and real remote lifecycle execution remain boundaries, not completed claims.

| one root `dx` manifest | partial | `[package]`, `[forge]`, `[[forge.files]]`, `[[forge.exports]]`, and `[forge.install]` feed local publish and selected add planning | no full CLI init/editor workflow yet |

| local registry | real local proof | `.dx/forge/registry/local` stores index, manifests, content-addressed files, integrity checks, publish receipts, selected-export installs, green-only updates, archive-backed removes, and `dx forge status --json` verification of matching root packages with canonical `.dx/receipts/forge/status-latest.json` output | remote registry lifecycle and richer conflict UX remain pending |
| R2/S3 registry | dry-run boundary | redacted object-key plan, env readiness, partial-credential readiness booleans, bucket/endpoint/public-base-url readiness booleans, setup status, missing-config labels, setup-derived next actions, dry-run add/update/remove lifecycle plans, read-only provider read plans for latest/manifest/content objects, fixture-backed manifest install previews, exact object metadata HEAD-check plans, approval-gated HEAD execution receipt shapes, and remote HEAD health evaluations exist without printing secrets or config values | no approved live root-package upload proof, real remote manifest fetch, live object HEAD/GET execution, blob fetch, or remote install/update/uninstall execution |
| remove/rollback safety | real local proof | rollback restores from trusted receipts; remove archives clean files before deletion and blocks local edits | restore-from-remove archive is manual/receipt-guided today |

## Live QA Checkpoint - 2026-05-21

- Live command path tested: `dx-www new target\live-qa\dx-launch-live-20260521-203450`, then `dx-www dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000` from the generated app.
- Confirmed the generated app kept the DX/Forge path no-install: no local `node_modules` folder was created.
- Confirmed `/_dx/hot-reload/version` responds with the hot-reload poll token and `/api/health` reports `{"nodeModulesRequired":false,"ok":true,"runtime":"dx-www"}`.
- Runtime blocker: `/launch` renders the generic fallback shell with the literal heading `{children}` instead of the composed `LaunchShell`; the live DOM has zero `data-dx-*` route/edit markers, zero forms, zero canvas elements, and none of the auth, payment, state/query, docs, automations, database, or Studio edit surfaces visible.
- Runtime blocker: `/automations`, `/ui`, `/database`, and `/backend` are advertised by the Studio preview manifest, but the generated app returned 404 for those routes in this live run.
- Runtime partial: `POST /api/checkout` safely returns the Stripe-shaped contact fallback without real payment data; `GET /api/checkout` correctly reports that the route does not export `GET`.
- Runtime blocker: `app/api/ai/chat/route.ts` exports `const POST = createDxAIChatRoute(...)`, but the current route handler detection reported that the route does not export `POST`; this needs compiler support for exported handler constants.
- Runtime blocker: the dynamic tRPC route file exists at `app/api/trpc/[trpc]/route.ts`, but `/api/trpc/health` returned 404 in this live run.
- Live website launch readiness is therefore source-rich but runtime-blocked. The next engineering target is App Router TSX/component rendering plus route materialization for the Studio manifest routes, not more catalog metadata.

## Live Runtime Recovery Checkpoint - 2026-05-21

- Live command path tested: `dx-www new target\live-qa\dx-launch-runtime-20260521 --template launch`, `node tools\launch\materialize-www-template.ts target\live-qa\dx-launch-runtime-20260521`, then `dx-www dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000` from the generated app.
- Runtime recovery: `/launch` now renders a DX-owned runtime page instead of the generic `{children}` fallback. The live DOM contains `data-dx-route="/launch"`, 26 Studio/Forge `data-dx-*` markers, one form, one canvas, auth/session, Stripe payment boundary, Zod-shaped validation, Zustand-style state, TanStack Query dashboard read-model refresh, Motion transition proof, docs/markdown, automations/n8n, database/backend, AI/WASM, and Studio edit markers.
- Runtime route recovery: `/automations`, `/ui`, `/database`, and `/backend` now return 200 as intentional DX WWW runtime pages with route markers instead of 404.
- Runtime API recovery: the materialized app writes function-export route handlers for `/api/auth/session`, `/api/checkout`, `/api/ai/chat`, and `/api/trpc/health` so the current safe route-handler interpreter can execute them without package installs.
- Live interactions verified in the in-app browser: sign-in state, payment boundary check, invalid and valid form validation, two state increments, health query refresh, Motion toggle, tRPC health, and AI adapter route all responded visibly.
- Live website launch readiness recovered to 76/100. Demanding truth: this is launch-demo usable for the DX/Forge thesis, but it is still a runtime materialization bridge, not full TSX/App Router component rendering parity yet.
- Coding follow-up: the source-only launch runtime materializer now copies no-`node_modules` `.html` pages for `/launch`, `/automations`, `/ui`, `/database`, and `/backend`, copies the runtime CSS/JS assets, writes `public/preview-manifest.json` with the embedded `dx.studio.launch_edit_contract` editable-surface and operation contract, and moves the fallback `app/page.tsx` to `.source-only`. `/launch` now has a source-owned operating dashboard that summarizes session, payment, automation, and database/backend state from the runtime interactions instead of only showing package proof cards. The focused materializer guard is green for the source/runtime bridge contract; this is not a live server/browser evidence claim.

## Urgent Live-Readiness Fix Pass - 2026-05-22

- Source fix scope after the 68/100 runtime QA: the Forge registry now maps `js/openapi/dx-launch.yaml` through `js/openapi -> openapi`, generated launch starters write the TSX `/launch` App Router route/components before Forge package slices, and the generated root `dx` file advertises `forge.paths.js_openapi="openapi"`.
- Runtime compatibility scope: DX-WWW dev now maps `/favicon.svg` to `public/favicon.svg`, and the no-`node_modules` launch materializer copies both root and public favicon fallbacks plus a `pages/favicon.svg.html` bridge for the current route-only runtime path.
- Browser-readiness scope: the runtime launch CSS adds mobile overflow guards for narrow 390px viewports, the icon materialization path preserves stable `data-dx-icon` and `data-icon-source="dx-icons"` markers even when `<dx-icon>` becomes SVG, and the scene runtime annotates canvas pixel samples with `data-dx-scene-pixel-proof`.
- Current binary status: `target/debug/dx-www.exe` has been rebuilt from the current source. A generation smoke confirmed `dx-www new --template launch` now writes `app/page.tsx`, `components/launch/launch-shell.tsx`, `openapi/dx-launch.yaml`, and `public/favicon.svg` without the old Forge path-mapping failure.

## Launch TSX Route Correction - 2026-05-22

- Runtime correction: `dx-www dev` now has a scoped TSX App Router launch responder for generated `LaunchShell` routes, so `/` and `/launch` can render the launch dashboard without leaking literal `{children}`.
- Product truth: the launch story is `.tsx` first. Legacy compatibility lanes remain internal and are not the public authoring path for new template work.

The workflow publishes `LaunchAutomationDashboardState` into `examples/template/automation-mission-summary.tsx` so `data-dx-component="launch-automation-mission-summary"` updates the real mission dashboard with selected connector, auth schema, credential types, required env, workflow-node readiness, and `data-dx-automation-dashboard-state`, while `launch-shell.tsx` stays focused on dashboard composition.
- Receipt seed: `G:\Dx\.dx\receipts\automations\launch-release-notification.json` is materialized as a redacted draft receipt with `runtime_execution: false`, empty `secret_values`, the launch workflow selector, auth schema/workflow-node readiness, and the CLI/Zed handoff commands. It is not live n8n evidence.
- Starter dashboard proof: `examples/dashboard/src/lib/n8nAutomationBridge.ts` and `examples/dashboard/src/components/AutomationWorkflowPanel.tsx` consume the same source-owned package shape with `data-dx-package="automations/n8n"`, connector selection, source-file provenance, credential-boundary readiness, `<dx-icon name="pack:workflow" />`, and a safe local redacted receipt action.
- Zed/DX Studio discovery: `dx www preview-manifest --json` now maps `automations/n8n` to the full `launch-automation-dashboard-workflow` section, the `launch-automation-connector-workflow` connector readiness surface, the `launch-automation-catalog-summary` metrics surface, and the `launch-automation-mission-summary` dashboard card, with `data-dx-dashboard-workflow`, `data-dx-automation-workflow="connector-readiness"`, `data-dx-automation-dashboard-state`, `data-dx-automation-dashboard-card`, `data-dx-automation-intent-input`, `data-dx-automation-receipt-path`, `data-dx-automation-run-receipt-path`, `data-dx-automation-required-env`, `data-dx-automation-credential-schema`, `data-dx-automation-auth-kind`, `data-dx-automation-credential-type`, `data-dx-automation-workflow-node-readiness`, and `data-dx-automation-usable-as-tool` markers so Web Preview selection opens the product workflow rather than the inner status widget.
- Package discovery: `dx forge packages --json` and the launch package catalog now list the launch shell, `automations-status.tsx`, materialized runtime page/script, starter-dashboard bridge/model files, and package docs so Zed can open the real dashboard workflow source path from the package record.
- API naming: package catalog and Studio discovery now point at the real `createDxN8nRunReceipt` helper from `js/lib/automations/n8n/receipt.ts`; the automations bridge guard rejects the older invented `createDxAutomationRunReceipt` metadata name.
- Component surface naming: `LaunchAutomationBridgeStatus` exposes `surface?: "dashboard" | "connector-readiness"`, defaults to `connector-readiness`, and uses that workflow id for safe local dry-run receipts so the source API matches the product workflow instead of proof/preview terminology.
- Receipt intent wiring: `createDxN8nRunReceipt` and starter-dashboard `createRedactedN8nReceipt` now preserve the normalized workflow intent as structured redacted receipt data. `/launch`, the runtime-safe bridge, the package catalog, and Studio metadata expose `data-dx-automation-receipt-intent` and `data-dx-automation-run-receipt-intent` so Zed can map edited intent text to the local and run-handoff receipts.
- Required-env wiring: `buildDxN8nCredentialReadiness`, `requiredEnvForDxN8nConnector`, `createDxN8nRunReceipt`, the starter dashboard, `/launch`, the no-`node_modules` runtime bridge, package catalog, Studio metadata, and `dx automations run --json` now carry `required_env` / `data-dx-automation-required-env` as a redacted app-owned boundary.
- CLI/Zed handoff: `dx automations connectors --json`, `dx automations credentials --json`, and `dx automations run --json` write redacted receipts under `G:\Dx\.dx\receipts\automations`. The run receipt is now aligned to the `/launch` workflow with `package_id: "automations/n8n"`, `dashboard_workflow: "automation-release-receipt"`, `source_selector: "[data-dx-component=\"launch-automation-dashboard-workflow\"]"`, the `launch-release-notification.json` seed path, `runtime_execution: false`, empty `secret_values`, and matching browser-visible `data-dx-automation-safe-action="prepare-zed-run-handoff"` markers.
- The Rust/Zed `run` bridge now copies the redacted `workflow_intent` object from `G:\Dx\.dx\receipts\automations\launch-release-notification.json` into the run receipt output when available, with a safe static fallback and no credential reads.
- Runtime policy: the bridge is metadata-first and redacted; live workflow execution stays blocked behind explicit operator approval and real credential wiring.
- Launch proof: `/launch` now imports the Forge automations catalog/readiness/receipt APIs as a product dashboard workflow rather than a package-card-only proof, keeps Slack/Notion credential needs honest, exposes selected auth schemas and workflow-node readiness in the browser source, and stays no-`node_modules`.

## Package Slices

- `auth/better-auth`: official **Authentication** source-owned slice based on upstream Better Auth `1.6.11` for `betterAuth`, `createAuthClient`, `toNextJsHandler`, `nextCookies`, server-side `auth.api.getSession({ headers })`, social sign-in helpers over `authClient.signIn.social`, linked-account helpers over `authClient.listAccounts`, `linkSocial`, `unlinkAccount`, and `getAccessToken`, profile/email-change helpers over `authClient.updateUser` and `authClient.changeEmail`, confirmation-gated account-deletion helpers over `authClient.deleteUser`, email/password sign-in and sign-up helpers, password reset/change helpers, verification-email helpers, server/client session-list, and session-revocation helpers, env contracts, and package-discovery metadata. Add with `dx add authentication --write`; compatibility aliases `better-auth`, `auth/betterauth`, and `auth/better-auth-next` canonicalize to the same Forge package while the user-facing package name remains Authentication. The launch-template dashboard workflow lives in `examples/template/auth-session-status.tsx` with real GitHub/Google sign-in and link-account actions, email sign-in and sign-up actions, profile/email-change form, password-reset request form, guarded account-deletion request form, guarded active-session refresh, linked-account refresh, and revoke-other-sessions actions. Applications still own the database adapter, session policy, trusted origins, OAuth provider credentials/redirect consent, account-linking policy/UX, token encryption/storage/use, profile-field policy, email delivery/verification/callback policy, reset/change-password UX, delete-account email delivery, delete-token expiry, before/after deletion hooks, account export UX, session-management UX, and production auth policy.
- Better Auth metadata note: generated `auth/better-auth/metadata.ts` now carries aliases, `G:\WWW\inspirations\better-auth` source mirror provenance, exported files, required env, app-owned boundaries, and Forge receipt paths; `examples/template/auth-session-status.tsx` reads that contract for visible package/source/receipt markers in `/launch`.
- Authentication dashboard account workflow: generated `auth/better-auth/dashboard.ts` and `examples/dashboard/src/lib/forge/auth/better-auth/dashboard.ts` expose source-owned session snapshot, profile update request, provider-linking readiness, and session-revocation receipt helpers based on upstream better-auth APIs. `examples/dashboard/src/components/BetterAuthAccountWorkflow.tsx`, the public App Router `/launch` route, the launch shell, and the existing runtime bridge now render them with `data-dx-package="auth/better-auth"`, a first-class `data-dx-component="better-auth-account-dashboard-workflow"` / `data-dx-section="account-access-dashboard"` outside the proof grid, a distinct `data-dx-component="better-auth-session-status-panel"` inner selector, visible `signUpDxBetterAuthEmail` email sign-up action markers, current no-`node_modules` runtime markers for `email-sign-up` and `preview-local-session`, a centralized runtime auth-state marker writer, a safe local account-session preview marked as `data-dx-auth-session-source="local-preview"`, a `data-dx-auth-network-state` missing-config gate for safe local auth previews and signed-in profile/account-deletion/linked-account/session actions, official docs at `docs/packages/authentication.md`, DX icon metadata, source mirror, upstream package provenance, required env, missing-provider readiness, and explicit app-owned database/OAuth/email/session policy boundaries.
- Better Auth dashboard receipt: `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/auth-better-auth.json` is the source-owned no-execution receipt for the `/launch` Better Auth workflow. It records upstream APIs, source and materialized files, stable Web Preview markers, required env, safe local-session preview behavior, app-owned production boundaries, and no-template-local-`node_modules` policy. The launch route contract and template surface registry name this exact receipt so generated `/launch` handoff discovery cannot silently lose it; it is guarded by `benchmarks/better-auth-dashboard-workflow.test.ts`.
- Better Auth generated-starter handoff: `dx new` now embeds the same receipt through `NEXT_FAMILIAR_BETTER_AUTH_DASHBOARD_RECEIPT_JSON`, writes `.dx/forge/receipts/auth-better-auth.json`, includes it in generated launch source/readiness metadata, and advertises the Better Auth aliases, source mirror, exported files, dashboard usage, DX icon, required env, and exact receipt paths in CLI package metadata.
- Better Auth CLI catalog provenance: `dx templates --json` / launch package metadata now advertises the complete source-owned Better Auth file set, including account deletion, route handlers, `.env.example`, README, dashboard workflow component, exact receipt paths, and curated provenance from the local `G:\WWW\inspirations\better-auth` mirror.
