- The Bangla locale lane now contains real Bangla dashboard copy in the source component, source-owned copy/receipt contract, generated Forge messages, runtime-safe `/launch` bridge, and targeted UTF-8 copy-integrity guard instead of English strings labeled as Bangla.
- The next-intl four-pass finishing audit is recorded as REAL for coding readiness in `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-i18n-next-intl-dashboard-locale.json`; remaining partial work is limited to governed browser proof, production locale routing verification, SEO alternate-link review, and runtime dependency verification.
- `content/fumadocs-next`: Fumadocs Next App Router source-owned docs slice for `createMDX`, `defineDocs`, the Fumadocs source loader, source plugins (`lucideIconsPlugin`, `statusBadgesPlugin`, `slugsFromData`), navigation helpers (`getBreadcrumbItems`, `flattenTree`, `findNeighbour`, `getPageTreePeers`), TOC helpers (`getTableOfContents`, `TOCItemType`, `page.data.toc`), `llms(source)` LLM index routes, processed Markdown page exports from `page.data.getText("processed")`, OpenAPI virtual docs/proxy with `createOpenAPI()`, `staticSource()`, `loaderPlugin()`, `createProxy()`, `proxyUrl`, `createAPIPage()`, `createCodeUsageGeneratorRegistry()`, `registerDefault()`, and `defineClientConfig()`, `createFromSource` dynamic search route wiring, `staticGET` static search-index export, `useDocsSearch` fetch/static client presets, docs layout/page primitives, MDX component wiring, starter docs/OpenAPI content, typed `/docs`, `/docs/api`, `/api/openapi/proxy`, `/llms.txt`, `/llms-full.txt`, `/llms.mdx/docs/[[...slug]]`, `/api/search`, and `/api/search-static` route contracts, and DX/Zed discovery metadata. Add with `dx add content/fumadocs-next --write` or the `fumadocs` alias. Applications still own dependency installation, existing Next config merge review, content governance, source plugin taxonomy, icon naming, status lifecycle, docs navigation policy, toc policy, slug/canonical URL policy, OpenAPI schema governance, OpenAPI proxy allowed origins via `DX_FUMADOCS_OPENAPI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`, auth/cookie forwarding policy, request code sample policy, AI indexing policy, private content exclusion, search UI, static-index payload budget, multilingual/vector search policy, deployment policy, and governed runtime verification.
- Markdown & MDX Content (`content/react-markdown`): source-owned launch slice with upstream provenance from `react-markdown` `10.1.0`, `@mdx-js/mdx` `3.1.1`, and `@mdx-js/react` `3.1.1`. It materializes the default `Markdown` export, `MarkdownAsync`, `MarkdownHooks`, `defaultUrlTransform`, `Components`, `Options`, safe default element filtering that still respects app-owned `disallowedElements`, component overrides, `MDXProvider`, `useMDXComponents`, `compile`, `compileSync`, `createProcessor`, `nodeTypes`, dx-check visibility metadata, dx-style markers, and Zed surface markers. Add with `dx add markdown-mdx-content --write`; `react-markdown`, `markdown/react`, `markdown/mdx`, and `mdx/content` remain provenance aliases. Applications still own dependency installation, content moderation, raw HTML policy, trusted MDX execution, remark/rehype plugin choices, and link governance.

## Discovery Commands

- `dx www preview-manifest --json` exposes `dx.zed.web_preview_contract` for Zed Web Preview: project detection signals, route picker fields, DOM marker names, a CSS-selector-ready `data_dx_marker_index`, a no-execution `source_guard_index`, a source-only `source_guard_runbook_index` with exact commands/contracts per route, a route-scoped `preview_watch_index`, `route_readiness_index`, `forge_readiness_index`, `source_selection_index`, `editable_surface_index`, `edit_operation_index`, `env_contract_index`, `forge_receipt_index`, semantic actions, the source-owned `dx.studio.launch_edit_contract` edit contract, insert component/move-reorder section/token/text/media operations, hot-reload targets, package-readiness badges, DOM-to-source selection, app-owned environment boundary badges, source-owned receipt/provenance pointers, rendered-proof acceptance/import/runtime-approval/completeness proof tools for converted routes, and a no-execution/no-`node_modules` safety contract.
- `examples/template/dx-studio-edit-contract.ts` is the source-owned `/launch` edit manifest for Zed/DX Studio operations: insert component, move/reorder section, update design token, update text/content, and insert icon/media. Its editable surface selectors point at the source files that own the rendered `data-dx-*` markers, the contract is responsive-grid/design-token aware, forbids absolute positioning, and keeps generated templates on the DX/Forge no-`node_modules` path.
- `dx forge packages --json` exposes package metadata plus the shared `dx.discovery` contract for DX CLI and Zed discovery.
- `dx automations connectors --json`, `dx automations credentials --json`, and `dx automations run --json` expose the n8n-sourced automation connector catalog, credential/auth-schema catalog, and receipt-only run readiness for Rust/Zed callers without starting providers or exporting secrets.
- `dx templates --json` exposes the `next-familiar-launch` template metadata plus shadcn button/card/field/item/input/textarea, the real `LaunchShadcnDashboardControls` dashboard workflow and receipt, Lucide React, Motion, 3D launch scene, Supabase, Drizzle, TanStack Query, React Hook Form, Stripe.js local preview receipts, no-`node_modules` tRPC contract proof, automations/n8n, InstantDB aliases/source mirror/exported dashboard files/receipt paths/DX icon metadata, wasm-bindgen, Zustand, next-intl, Fumadocs, and react-markdown launch usage examples without executing package installs. Its shared `dx.discovery` contract, `dx.zed.template_handoff` handoff, `dx.launch.readiness_bundle` bundle, and `/launch` entrypoint metadata record the source route, materialized route, generated-template file manifest, safe source checks, package/template guard evidence, open-file map, readiness receipt, readiness bundle file, and companion launch component files needed by the generated App Router surface.
- `dx templates verify-readiness --project <path> --json` and `dx forge template-readiness --project <path> --json` read the generated `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-route.json` receipt, verify materialized files/package catalog coverage, and keep runtime proof permission-gated.
- `dx forge launch-readiness-bundle --project <path> --json` reads the generated starter evidence and returns a no-execution bundle for DX CLI/Zed: template readiness, package receipt presence, companion proof docs, runtime checklist gates, source guard declarations, handoff metadata, and the permission-gated runtime status.
- `dx forge launch-adoption-report --project <path> --json` reads the generated template manifest and readiness receipt to summarize materialized launch companion files, app-owned package/env boundaries, and the explicit runtime-proof gate without starting a server, installing packages, or running a full build.
- `dx forge launch-manifest-drift --project <path> --json` compares the generated template manifest, readiness receipt, readiness bundle, and companion-file coverage before runtime verification without starting a server, installing packages, or running a full build.
- `dx forge launch-companion-receipts --project <path> --json` verifies generated companion proof docs for the launch shell package slices so DX CLI/Zed can open source, materialized, and package documentation proof paths without runtime work.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-checklist --project <path> --json` reads `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-checklist.json` and reports the explicit approval gate for build, preview, and final runtime evidence collection without executing any runtime command.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-approval-request --project <path> --json` reads `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-approval-request.json` and reports the pending approval record, requested commands, and requested evidence receipts without pre-approving or executing runtime work.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence --project <path> --json` reads `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-evidence.json` and reports the future required route-response, production-preview, and final launch receipt artifacts as not collected without creating fake runtime proof.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-import-plan --project <path> --build-log <path> --route-response <path> --preview-proof <path> --json` requires the runtime approval request to be approved, then maps externally collected evidence files to stable `.dx/forge/runtime` targets with source paths, byte sizes, and BLAKE3 hashes, without copying files or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-completeness --project <path> --import-plan <path> --json` reads the launch verification lane, runtime evidence contract, and import-plan receipt, then validates supplied runtime evidence source paths, BLAKE3 hashes, stale-source matches, and declared `.dx/forge/runtime` targets without copying files or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-finalize --project <path> --import-plan <path> --write --json` writes `.dx/forge/runtime/final-launch-evidence-receipt.json` and updates `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-evidence.json` only after completeness and current source-hash checks pass, without copying evidence files or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-review --project <path> --json` reads the finalized runtime evidence contract and finalization receipt, recomputes source hashes for tamper detection, and reports final launch-evidence readiness without copying files or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-packet --project <path> --json` reads the readiness bundle, template/source manifests, source receipts, finalization receipt, and final review report, then reports BLAKE3 packet-integrity metadata for `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-packet.json` without copying runtime artifacts or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-index --project <path> --json` lists the launch evidence sequence, expected files, required inputs, rerun commands, stale-step hints, and release-packet status for `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-operator-index.json` without reading runtime artifact contents or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-status-timeline --project <path> --json` summarizes the latest completed launch-evidence step, next blocked step, release readiness, and operator-index/release-packet timestamp freshness for `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-status-timeline.json` without reading runtime artifact contents or running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-handoff-digest --project <path> --write` writes a Zed-openable Markdown digest at `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-handoff-digest.md` from the status timeline, operator index, release packet, and final runtime review using file metadata only.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-release-checklist --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-release-checklist.json` as the final no-execution launch signoff checklist from the handoff digest, status timeline, operator index, and release packet.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-share-manifest --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-share-manifest.json` as a DX CLI/Zed export manifest for the checklist, digest, timeline, operator index, and packet handoff paths.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-archive-index --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-archive-index.json` as the long-term launch archive handoff index for the share manifest, checklist, digest, and packet.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-archive-receipt --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-archive-receipt.json` as the final no-execution archive receipt for the archive index and operator handoff target.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-archive-ledger --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-archive-ledger.json` as the durable release ledger for archive receipt, archive index, share manifest, checklist, digest, and packet paths.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-retention-policy --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-retention-policy.json` as the no-execution keep/refresh/regenerate policy after archive ledger handoff.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-retention-review --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-retention-review.json` as the no-execution review of the retention policy against the archive ledger and release proof chain.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-release-seal --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-release-seal.json` as the final no-execution launch handoff seal for the retention review, retention policy, archive ledger, and release packet.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-summary --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-operator-summary.json` as a terminal-friendly no-execution handoff summary over the release seal, retention review, release packet, and final runtime review.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-completion-ledger --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-completion-ledger.json` as the final no-execution launch evidence completion map over the operator summary, release seal, retention review, and final runtime review.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-closure-memo --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-closure-memo.md` as a Zed-openable no-execution Markdown closeout over the completion ledger, operator summary, release seal, and final runtime review.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-final-brief --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-final-brief.json` as the final no-execution DX CLI/Zed launch closeout pointer over the closure memo, completion ledger, operator summary, and release seal.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-runbook --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-operator-runbook.json` as a restartable no-execution DX worker handoff checklist over the final brief, closure memo, completion ledger, operator summary, and release seal.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-handoff-capsule --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-handoff-capsule.json` as the compact no-execution restart artifact over the operator runbook, final brief, closure memo, and completion ledger.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-resumption-index --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-resumption-index.json` as an ordered no-execution restart-lane map for source-only, runtime-approved, and release-closeout follow-up work.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-recovery-brief --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-recovery-brief.md` as a Zed-openable no-execution Markdown restart brief from the resumption index.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-continuation-packet --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-continuation-packet.json` as a compact no-execution DX CLI/Zed restart packet over the recovery brief, resumption index, handoff capsule, and operator runbook.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-resume-card --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-operator-resume-card.json` as a terminal-first no-execution status card from the continuation packet.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-ledger --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-ledger.json` as a durable no-execution restart ledger over the operator resume card, continuation packet, recovery brief, and resumption index.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-checklist --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-checklist.json` as a no-execution next-action checklist over the restart ledger, with source-only, runtime-approved, and release-closeout lanes.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-brief --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-brief.md` as a Zed-openable no-execution Markdown handoff from the restart checklist.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-manifest --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-manifest.json` as an indexable no-execution restart manifest over the restart brief, restart checklist, restart ledger, and operator resume card.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-receipt --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-receipt.json` as the latest resumable no-execution DX CLI/Zed handoff from the restart manifest.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-summary --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-summary.json` as a terminal-friendly no-execution DX CLI/Zed handoff over the restart receipt, restart manifest, restart brief, and restart checklist.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-snapshot --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-snapshot.json` as the latest-openable no-execution DX CLI/Zed restart file over the restart summary, restart receipt, restart manifest, and restart brief.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-dispatch --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-dispatch.json` as a one-command no-execution DX CLI/Zed next-worker dispatch card over the restart snapshot, summary, receipt, and manifest.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-closeout --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-closeout.md` as a final no-execution DX CLI/Zed Markdown closeout over the restart dispatch with Friday and essencefromexistence operator actions.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-signoff --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-restart-signoff.json` as a reviewable no-execution DX CLI/Zed acceptance receipt over the restart closeout with Friday and essencefromexistence acceptance metadata.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-acceptance-index --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-acceptance-index.md` as a no-execution DX CLI/Zed Markdown index over restart signoff, closeout, dispatch, and snapshot artifacts for Friday's final handoff.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-acceptance-digest --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-acceptance-digest.json` as a terminal-first no-execution DX CLI/Zed final status line over the acceptance index for Friday.
- `dx forge launch-evidence-friday-baton --project <path> --write` writes `.dx/forge/release/launch-evidence-friday-baton.md` as a no-execution orchestrator handoff over the acceptance digest, acceptance index, restart signoff, and launch verification lane for Friday.
- `dx forge launch-verification-lane --project <path> --json` reads `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-verification-lane.json` and reports the operator-facing checklist, approval request, and runtime evidence sequence for DX CLI/Zed without running builds, previews, or servers.
- `dx new <name>` materializes the no-install `/launch` route surface into `app/page.tsx`, `components/launch/*`, `components/local/LaunchConsole.tsx`, `server/launchCatalog.ts`, `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-route.json`, `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-readiness-bundle.json`, `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-checklist.json`, `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-approval-request.json`, `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-runtime-evidence.json`, `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-verification-lane.json`, `.dx/forge/docs/dx-www-starter-ui.md`, and the launch-template Forge receipt docs so the generated starter has real launch-route source, summary counts, and review evidence before any package install.
- `dx new <name>` now also materializes the launch package slices used by the shell: shadcn badge/label/separator/field/item/input/textarea, icons, auth, motion, 3D launch scene, i18n, query, validation, forms, payments, automations/n8n, state, AI, tRPC, Fumadocs, react-markdown, Supabase, Drizzle, InstantDB, and wasm-bindgen, including the Forms, Automation Connectors, Drizzle, next-intl, Fumadocs, and InstantDB dashboard workflow receipts under `.dx/forge/receipts`. It still does not create `node_modules`; package files and receipts stay source-owned.
- `dx new <name>` writes `.dx/forge/template-manifest.json` with a `generated_files` map and launch `component_materialized_files` list from source examples to materialized route, component, runtime, and readiness files for DX CLI/Zed discovery.
- The generated `/launch` surface is guarded with `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-shell.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-package-slices.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli www_templates_json_reports_launch_template_metadata`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_packages_json_reports_launch_discovery_metadata`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli dx_new_materializes_every_launch_manifest_source_file`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_launch_readiness_bundle_aggregates_generated_project_evidence_without_runtime`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_launch_runtime_checklist_requires_explicit_approval_without_execution`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_launch_runtime_approval_request_reports_pending_approval_without_execution`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_launch_runtime_evidence_reports_required_runtime_artifacts_without_fake_proof`, and `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_launch_verification_lane_reports_operator_sequence_without_runtime`; runtime route verification is a separate governed pass that requires explicit permission before any build or preview command is run.
- Source/generated-template readiness stays separate from runtime approval artifacts: `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_readiness_reports_stay_green_without_runtime_approval_artifacts` verifies readiness reports remain green when optional approval/evidence/lane artifacts are absent, while `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli approved_runtime_evidence_report_requires_real_collected_artifacts` verifies approved runtime evidence fails without real collected proof.
- `examples/template/package-catalog.ts` is the source-side launch template catalog example for package roles, professional front-facing names, source API surfaces, `dx add` commands, required env vars, and app-owned production boundaries.
- `examples/template/launch-route-contract.ts` is the source-side route contract that keeps `/launch`, the typed materialized-file list, readiness summary counts, the template-readiness receipt path, source smoke checks, and permission-gated runtime-verification request/status discoverable.
- `examples/template/launch-shell.tsx` is the composed launch dashboard shell. It consumes the catalog with auth session status, AI chat status, data adapters, payments, docs, state, TanStack Query cache status, forms, Zod validation status, Zod dashboard settings validation, Lucide icon status, next-intl launch status, the next-intl dashboard locale workflow, InstantDB realtime status, wasm-bindgen interop status, UI primitives, Motion provider/controlled status/frame ticker/page visibility/will-change performance hints/LazyMotion feature boundary/shared-layout/MotionValue meter/presence/reorder/reveal/scroll progress, markdown content rendering, automation bridge metadata, DX Studio edit-contract selectors, package role summaries, app-owned boundaries, app-owned submit handling, and required-env reporting.
- `examples/template/data-status.tsx` proves Drizzle schema, typed views, relational-query, typed-join, set-operation, CTE/subquery, transaction, prepared-query, conflict-write, mutation, aggregate analytics, and migration metadata, Supabase public-env validation, and InstantDB metadata can coexist behind a real data boundary with a safe missing-config state.
- `examples/template/payments-status.tsx` proves Stripe.js public-key validation and an app-owned Checkout Session contact boundary without collecting card fields or pretending to process a payment without secrets.
- `examples/template/docs-status.tsx` powers the `/launch` docs/help/changelog dashboard workflow with Fumadocs route-contract discovery, source plugin frontmatter discovery, navigation snapshot discovery, TOC summary discovery, LLMs route discovery, OpenAPI proxy/config discovery, dynamic/static search discovery, client search preset metadata, safe React Markdown rendering, `data-dx-component="launch-fumadocs-docs-workflow"`, `data-dx-dashboard-workflow="docs-help-changelog"`, and a safe local route receipt action. `examples/template/launch-route-contract.ts` records the same workflow receipt and its generated materialized receipt path for Zed/DX Studio handoff.
- `examples/template/launch-scene.tsx` is the source-owned WebGL scene boundary used by the launch shell and materialized with the `3d/launch-scene` Forge slice; it now uses DX token surfaces plus DX Icons metadata instead of hardcoded package-brand colors.
- `examples/template/auth-session-status.tsx` is the standalone Better Auth account/session workflow consumed by the launch shell, public App Router `/launch` route, and materialized `components/launch/auth-session-status.tsx`.
- `examples/template/ai-chat-status.tsx` is the standalone AI SDK client-chat proof consumed by the launch shell and materialized into `components/launch/ai-chat-status.tsx`; Vercel AI SDK remains provenance metadata.
- `examples/template/instantdb-status.tsx` is the InstantDB realtime/presence workflow consumed by the launch shell and materialized into `components/launch/instantdb-status.tsx`; the live `/launch.html` bridge also exposes `data-dx-component="instantdb-runtime-dashboard-workflow"` with a safe local receipt action, and `dx new` writes the matching `.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-instantdb-realtime-dashboard.json` receipt so InstantDB remains visible and auditable in the generated `/launch` route.
- `examples/template/wasm-interop-status.tsx` is the standalone wasm-bindgen hook proof consumed by the real `/launch` operating dashboard local-compute workflow and materialized into `components/launch/wasm-interop-status.tsx`; it now carries DX-owned `pack:wasm-bindgen` icon metadata, a visible local WebAssembly add action, source-owned package markers, missing app-generated-module readiness, mission-control metric wiring, and metadata-backed app-owned boundaries/receipt paths.
- `examples/dashboard/src/components/WasmBindgenWorkflow.tsx` is the starter dashboard workflow for `wasm/bindgen`; it imports source-owned typed package metadata and receipt helpers, exposes `data-dx-component="dashboard-wasm-bindgen-workflow"`, records the `launch-runtime-wasm-compute-dashboard` preview surface handoff, uses `<dx-icon name="pack:wasm-bindgen" />`, and proves the package with a local WebAssembly action while generated wasm-bindgen glue stays app-owned.
- `examples/template/zod-validation-status.tsx` and `examples/template/zod-dashboard-settings.tsx` are the standalone Validation & Schemas validation, JSON Schema export/import, and dashboard settings workflow proofs consumed by the launch shell and materialized into `components/launch/*`; `zod` remains upstream provenance metadata from `G:/WWW/inspirations/zod`, with public APIs `safeParse`, `z.strictObject`, `z.flattenError`, `z.treeifyError`, `z.prettifyError`, `z.toJSONSchema`, and `z.fromJSONSchema`.
- Validation & Schemas lane update, 2026-05-22: `examples/template/package-catalog.ts`, `core/src/ecosystem/forge_zod.rs`, `docs/packages/validation-zod.md`, `examples/dashboard/src/lib/zodDashboardSettings.ts`, `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-validation-zod-dashboard-settings.json`, and `benchmarks/zod-dashboard-settings-workflow.test.ts` now separate the official DX package name from upstream `zod` provenance, carry `SOURCE-ONLY` receipt honesty, expose official CLI copy as `dx add validation-schemas --write`, and keep runtime proof deferred. Verified with `dx run --test .\benchmarks\zod-dashboard-settings-workflow.test.ts`; skipped builds, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and broad suites. Next action: wire the same official-package-name metadata into dx-check package status rows if a later worker opens the shared check surface.
- `examples/template/icon-status.tsx` is the DX Icons proof consumed by the launch shell and materialized into `components/launch/icon-status.tsx`.
- `examples/template/app/page.tsx` is the App Router materialization proof for the composed shell, using source-owned next-intl message loading, `DxIntlProvider`, and the markdown preview.
- `examples/template/react-markdown-preview.tsx` is the standalone markdown preview wedge consumed by the route mount and the `content/react-markdown` metadata.
- `examples/template/query-cache-status.tsx` is the standalone TanStack Query cache-status proof consumed by the launch shell and materialized into `components/launch/query-cache-status.tsx`; it now covers refresh mutation invalidation, imperative `fetchQuery` actions, dependent `skipToken` queries, placeholder data via `keepPreviousData`, structural sharing via `replaceEqualDeep`, query-result summaries via `UseQueryResult`, cache-control snapshots, core `Query`/`Mutation` state summaries, cache matching summaries via `matchQuery`/`matchMutation`, persisted-cache readiness via `PersistQueryClientProvider`/persist-client helpers, opt-in Devtools status via `ReactQueryDevtools`, cross-tab cache sync readiness via `broadcastQueryClient`, hydration policy summaries, `QueryCache` summary instrumentation, restore-state display from `useIsRestoring`, runtime manager status from `environmentManager`, cancellation error-kind handling from `isCancelledError`, default-policy summaries, and `QueryObserver` snapshot status.
- The same TanStack Query proof now includes a visible dashboard settings panel for focus/online lifecycle state. It toggles TanStack's real `focusManager` and `onlineManager` through source-owned helpers, exposes package markers for DX Studio, uses generated theme-token class surfaces, and uses DX icon syntax instead of external branding.
- `examples/template/trpc-launch-contract.ts` owns the tRPC launch route/procedure contract and local receipt helper, and `examples/template/trpc-launch-health.tsx` is the typed API health workflow used by the `api/trpc` package slice, the account/data dashboard, and `dx templates --json`; the runtime-safe `/launch` bridge also maps it to `launch-trpc-api-dashboard-workflow` in mission control.
- `benchmarks/launch-template-shell.test.ts` is the lightweight source-level guard for the composed shell; it checks catalog coverage, shell consumption, and CLI metadata without starting a server or running a full build. `benchmarks/launch-package-slices.test.ts` guards launch package registration, CLI metadata, scorecard/trust-policy coverage, ecosystem module wiring, and upstream public API markers without compiling the workspace. The targeted `dx_new_materializes_every_launch_manifest_source_file` Cargo guard checks that `dx new` writes every launch manifest file, carries the full package catalog into the generated starter, and keeps launch companion components listed for DX CLI/Zed discovery.

## TSX Composed Snapshot Step

Generic App Router source-render now emits `dx.tsx.composedStaticDomSnapshot` with a separate `schema_revision: 1`. The snapshot walks route/layout/template/page documents, keeps safe intrinsic JSX output, and replaces local source-owned component usages with the already-bounded `component_return_preview` HTML when the component is imported from local TSX/JSX. The JSON reports `composed_static_dom_snapshot_elements`, `component_preview_insertions`, `component_prop_identifier_bindings`, skipped component references, and explicit `full_component_execution: false`.

This is a real TSX compatibility improvement, not a external framework clone parity claim. It proves DX-WWW can connect route source, local component imports, literal props, children insertion, state reflection, and Studio-readable DOM evidence without `node_modules`. The next engine task is ordered child graphs plus a stricter safe component execution subset that can render into the visible response DOM.

Lightweight verification for this pass: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024 dx-www\src\cli\app_router_execution\source_render.rs`, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` with the existing unused `THREE_SCENE_OFFICIAL_PACKAGE_NAME` warning. Skipped: local servers, browser automation, full workspace builds, broad suites, package installs, deploys, and live React/browser QA.

## TSX Ordered Child Tree

The TSX lowering surface now preserves ordered JSX child structure through `parent_index` and `child_nodes`. Both the custom scanner and Oxc JSX backend populate the same shape, and the App Router source renderer uses it to emit nested intrinsic HTML and nested source-owned component previews rather than flattening every JSX element into sibling output.

This removes a real fake-React smell: `<main><Card><span>...</span></Card></main>` can now be represented as a nested compiler-owned DOM tree in the safe static/common subset. It still does not execute arbitrary component functions, effects, context, reducers, async data, or full React reconciliation.

Verification for this pass: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024` on touched TSX runtime files, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` with only the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning.

## Contract Naming Cleanup

The TSX/App Router runtime now avoids public generated version suffixes in the scoped framework code. Schemas use readable names such as `dx.tsx.appRouterSemantics`, `dx.tsx.stateRuntime`, `dx.tsx.sourceRenderSurface`, `dx.tsx.componentReturnPreview`, `dx.tsx.domActionBinder`, and `dx.framework.riskRegister`; revisioning lives in `schema_revision` or `contract_revision`.

This is the style we should keep: stable professional names, explicit numeric revision fields, and no generated-looking suffixes in docs or UI-facing contracts. Older Forge/launch receipt schemas still need a separate migration because they are consumed by more generated files and should not be renamed blindly during TSX runtime work.

## Check Policy

- Do not start dev servers.
- Do not run full builds without user permission.
- Use `git diff --check`, conflict-marker search, and targeted checks after coherent implementation milestones.

## dx-check Receipt Reader Stabilization

`core/src/ecosystem/dx_check_receipt.rs` now normalizes Zed quick-fix risk metadata before emitting the DX-WWW panel view model or returning the embedded Zed payload. This keeps older receipts honest by deriving risk from command shape while preserving explicit `risk_level`, `requires_user_approval`, and `writes_receipts` fields when present.

The single-owner stabilization pass also aligned package-lane metric helpers for receipt-hash freshness drift in Authentication, Documentation System, Internationalization, AI SDK, and related rows without changing unrelated launch behavior.

Verification: `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler dx_check_latest_panel_reads_zed_receipt --lib` passed. Skipped: full workspace builds, local servers, browser automation, deploys, and broad suites. Exact next action: split the large receipt reader into schema, quick-fix normalization, and package-lane helper modules after active workers settle.

## Database ORM Check-Panel Mirror Attribution

Lane 9 remains scoped to official **Database ORM** (`db/drizzle-sqlite`), with upstream provenance from `G:/WWW/inspirations/drizzle-orm` (`drizzle-orm` `0.45.3`). Re-inspected upstream source files: `drizzle-orm/package.json`, `drizzle-orm/src/sqlite-core/db.ts`, and `drizzle-orm/src/better-sqlite3/driver.ts`; the relevant public APIs remain `withReplicas`, `selectDistinct`, `$count`, and `drizzle`.

`core/src/ecosystem/dx_check_receipt.rs` now carries the Database ORM receipt-hash helper `mirror_problem_count` through `DxCheckPanelPackageLaneHashRefreshRow` alongside `current_files`, `stale_files`, `missing_files`, `stale_mirror_files`, and `missing_mirror_files`. This keeps Zed/DX Studio remediation specific when `docs/packages/database-orm.source-guard-runbook.json` or `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` drifts, without claiming live SQLite reads, migration execution, replica health, or browser visual proof.

Files changed in this pass: `core/src/ecosystem/dx_check_receipt.rs`, `benchmarks/drizzle-dx-check-package-lane-panel.test.ts`, `benchmarks/drizzle-package-status-read-model.test.ts`, `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-db-drizzle-sqlite-dashboard-workflow.json`, `examples/template/.dx/forge/package-status.json`, `examples/template/forge-package-status-read-model.ts`, `docs/packages/db-drizzle-sqlite.md`, `docs/DX_WWW_FRAMEWORK_STRUCTURE.md`, `DX.md`, `TODO.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md`. Targeted proof: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-dx-check-package-lane-panel.test.ts`, focused Database ORM guard bundle, `node examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts --write`, `node tools/launch/run-template-receipt-helper.js examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts --check --json`, syntax checks for touched Database ORM benchmarks, JSON parse checks for refreshed receipts, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict/trailing-whitespace scans. `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check .\core\src\ecosystem\dx_check_receipt.rs` is still blocked by unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in the large shared reader around the State Management test fixture. Heavy checks skipped by policy: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo compilation, and live SQLite runtime proof. Exact next action: add a tiny generated JSON fixture for a Database ORM mirror-only drift row so Studio tests can assert nonzero `mirror_problem_count` without mutating selected Drizzle source hashes.

## Database ORM Mirror-Only Drift Fixture

Lane 9 remains scoped to official **Database ORM** (`db/drizzle-sqlite`) with upstream provenance from `G:/WWW/inspirations/drizzle-orm` (`drizzle-orm` `0.45.3`). Re-inspected upstream `drizzle-orm/package.json`, `drizzle-orm/src/sqlite-core/db.ts`, and `drizzle-orm/src/better-sqlite3/driver.ts`; public APIs used remain `withReplicas`, `selectDistinct`, `$count`, and `drizzle`.

`examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts` now supports `--write-mirror-drift-fixture` and writes `docs/packages/database-orm.mirror-drift.fixture.json`. The fixture is SOURCE-ONLY evidence for the DX Studio/check-panel row: selected Drizzle/dashboard source hashes remain current, `stale_files` is empty, `stale_mirror_files` points at `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts`, and `mirror_problem_count` is `3` for the receipt, package-status, and read-model mirror entries. `docs/packages/db-drizzle-sqlite.md`, `docs/DX_WWW_FRAMEWORK_STRUCTURE.md`, and `examples/template/package-catalog.ts` now publish the fixture without claiming live SQLite, migration, replica, install, or browser proof.

Files changed in this pass: `examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts`, `docs/packages/database-orm.mirror-drift.fixture.json`, `benchmarks/drizzle-receipt-hash-refresh.test.ts`, `benchmarks/drizzle-dx-check-visibility-receipt.test.ts`, `docs/packages/db-drizzle-sqlite.md`, `docs/DX_WWW_FRAMEWORK_STRUCTURE.md`, `examples/template/package-catalog.ts`, `DX.md`, `TODO.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md`. ## Database ORM Preview Materializer Surface Regeneration

Lane 9 also now guards the Database ORM preview-manifest materializer surface against read-model drift. `examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts --write` recreates `database-orm-preview-manifest-materializer` in the dashboard workflow receipt, `package-status.json`, and `forge-package-status-read-model.ts` if that surface is removed while preserving the SOURCE-ONLY Database ORM boundary.

The regression lives in `benchmarks/drizzle-receipt-hash-refresh.test.ts` and removes the preview materializer surface from a temp receipt, package-status row, and typed read model before proving the helper restores matching hashes and returns to `current`. No live SQLite read, package install, server, browser, provider, deploy, or build proof is claimed.

## Forge Source-Owned Package Review Fixture Alignment

The shared Forge source-owned package review harness now uses Node's `fs.cpSync` when cloning local adoption scenarios, so the all-package update rehearsal runs on the supported filesystem API instead of a broken `fs.tsxSync` typo. The fixture's Authentication row also uses the canonical `auth-better-auth` docs and receipt slug for package id `auth/better-auth`, matching the real package-status/read-model lane instead of the stale `auth-google` fixture name.

Verification for this pass: green `node --test --test-concurrency=1 .\benchmarks\forge-source-owned-package-review.test.ts .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`, green full source-only launch cluster covering 91 tests across launch-template, launch-forge, maturity, golden-path, lucide exclusion, and source-owned review guards, green 20/20 package-status read-model sweep, and green 20/20 receipt-helper freshness audit. Browser route checks, provider execution, package installs, deploys, npm builds, and full Cargo builds remain approval-gated.
